---
title: "clinical_var"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "12/2/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)
library(ppcor)

library(reshape2)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
```


```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species1 = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/helios500_clinical.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Papaya", "Orange", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species1
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species1)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[2] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species1) * 0.5
species_filtered1 <- species1 %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)
species_filtered2 <- species1 %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species1) * 0.1)
# species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)
# 
# #abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
# #species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
# #species_filtered2
# species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
# #species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
# #species_filtered2
# food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
# #food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
# nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
unique_species <- read.csv("figure/unique_significant_species.csv")
unique_species$species
colnames(species1)[1] = "Participant_ID"
sig_species <- species1 %>%
  dplyr::select("Participant_ID", all_of(unique_species$species))

colnames(metadata)[2] = "Participant_ID"
```


```{r}
sig_species
metadata

sig_species <- sig_species %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))

metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))
```

```{r}
#species_filtered2
merged_data <- sig_species %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")
merged_data_all = merged_data %>%
  inner_join(food_data, by = "Participant_ID")
merged_data
metadata
```
# four groups based on bifido and thosai

```{r}
#
```

```{r}
#     sum(table["Other Groups", ])
#   
#   return(risk_high / risk_other)
# }
# 
# rr <- risk_ratio(high_high_table)
# print("Risk Ratio (High-High vs Others):")
# print(rr)
```

```{r}
# print(rr)
```






# heatmaps
```{r}
# Define columns
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
clinical_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "BMI", "HOMA_IR", "HOMA_B")

# Initialize matrices for correlations and p-values
cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))
p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))

# Calculate partial correlations
for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(clinical_vars)) {
    # Create temporary dataset with ranked variables
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data[[clinical_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    # Calculate partial correlation
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    # Cap correlation values at ±0.2
    cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
}

# Add row and column names
rownames(cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cor_matrix) <- clinical_vars
rownames(p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(p_matrix) <- clinical_vars

# Perform clustering
species_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Convert to long format
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cor$Significant <- melted_p$value < 0.05

# Set factor levels based on clustering
melted_cor$Species <- factor(melted_cor$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_cor$Clinical <- factor(melted_cor$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)
#melted_cor$Clinical
# Create the plot
clinical_labels <- c(
  "BMI" = "BMI",
  "DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent" = "HbaA1C",
  "DLAB83_CRP" = "CRP",
  "HOMA_IR" = "HOMA-IR",
  "HOMA_B" = "HOMA-B"
)

# Plot with updated x-axis labels
ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = clinical_labels) +  # Rename x-axis labels
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlations of Species and Clinical Variable",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Species"
  )

# Save the plot if needed
#ggsave("figure/fig6_clinical_species_correlations.pdf", width = 6, height = 11)
```
```{r}
melted_cor
p_matrix
```





```{r}
library(gridExtra)
merged_data <- sig_species %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Define columns
species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "BMI", "HOMA_IR", "HOMA_B")

# Initialize matrices for continuous variables
cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))

# Initialize vectors for diabetes status
diab_effect <- numeric(length(species_cols))
diab_p <- numeric(length(species_cols))

# Calculate correlations for continuous variables
for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species = rank(merged_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
  
  # Calculate diabetes association
  temp_data <- data.frame(
    species_abundance = merged_data[[species_cols[i]]],
    gender = merged_data$FREG7_Gender,
    age = merged_data$FREG8_Age,
    ethnicity = merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
    diabetes = merged_data$diabetes_status
  )
  
  model <- lm(species_abundance ~ gender + age + ethnicity, data = temp_data)
  species_residuals <- residuals(model)
  
  kw_test <- kruskal.test(species_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
  
  groups <- split(species_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
  means <- sapply(groups, mean)
  overall_mean <- mean(species_residuals)
  ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
  ss_total <- sum((species_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
  eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
  
  if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
    direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
  } else {
    direction <- 1
  }
  
  diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
  diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
}

# Prepare continuous variables plot
rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- species_cols
colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars

# Clustering for continuous variables
species_dist <- dist(cont_cor_matrix)
species_clust <- hclust(species_dist)
species_order <- species_clust$labels[species_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cont_cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Prepare data for plots
# Continuous variables
melted_cont <- melt(cont_cor_matrix)
melted_cont_p <- melt(cont_p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
melted_cont$Species <- factor(melted_cont$Species, levels = species_order)
melted_cont$Clinical <- factor(melted_cont$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)

# Diabetes status
diab_df <- data.frame(
  Species = species_cols,
  Effect = diab_effect,
  P_value = diab_p
)
diab_df$Species <- factor(diab_df$Species, levels = species_order)
diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05

# Create continuous variables plot
p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Species Correlations with Clinical Variables",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Species",
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

# Create diabetes status plot
p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Species, x = Effect)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(brewer.pal(9, "PuBu"))) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "plum4") +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Species Associations with Diabetes Status",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
  )

# Arrange plots side by side
grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1))

# Optionally save the combined plot
#ggsave("figure/clinical_diabetes_associations2.pdf", arrangeGrob(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1)), width = 15, height = 10)
```
## separate ethnicity
```{r}
library(gridExtra)

# Function to perform analysis for one ethnic group
analyze_ethnic_group <- function(merged_data, ethnic_group) {
  # Filter data for the specific ethnic group
  ethnic_data <- merged_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic_group)
  
  # Get columns
  species_cols <- colnames(sig_species)[colnames(sig_species) != "Participant_ID"]
  continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "BMI", "HOMA_IR", "HOMA_B")
  
  # Initialize matrices
  cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(species_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  diab_effect <- numeric(length(species_cols))
  diab_p <- numeric(length(species_cols))
  
  # Calculate correlations for continuous variables
  for(i in 1:length(species_cols)) {
    for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
      temp_data <- data.frame(
        species = rank(ethnic_data[[species_cols[i]]]),
        clinical = rank(ethnic_data[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
        gender = as.numeric(ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
        age = rank(ethnic_data$FREG8_Age)
      )
      
      # Now adjusting only for gender and age
      pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$species, temp_data$clinical,
                              temp_data[,c("gender", "age")])
      
      cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
      cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
    }
    
    # Calculate diabetes association
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      species_abundance = ethnic_data[[species_cols[i]]],
      gender = ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender,
      age = ethnic_data$FREG8_Age,
      diabetes = ethnic_data$diabetes_status
    )
    
    # Adjust for gender and age only
    model <- lm(species_abundance ~ gender + age, data = temp_data)
    species_residuals <- residuals(model)
    
    kw_test <- kruskal.test(species_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
    
    groups <- split(species_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
    means <- sapply(groups, mean)
    overall_mean <- mean(species_residuals)
    ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
    ss_total <- sum((species_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
    eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
    
    if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
      direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
    } else {
      direction <- 1
    }
    
    diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
    diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
  }
  
  # Prepare matrices
  rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- species_cols
  colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  
  # Return results
  list(
    cont_cor = cont_cor_matrix,
    cont_p = cont_p_matrix,
    diab_effect = diab_effect,
    diab_p = diab_p
  )
}

# Analyze each ethnic group
results_C <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "C")
results_I <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "I")
results_M <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data, "M")

# Function to create plots for one ethnic group
create_plots <- function(results, species_cols, continuous_vars, group_name) {
  # Prepare continuous variables plot
  melted_cont <- melt(results$cont_cor)
  melted_cont_p <- melt(results$cont_p)
  colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Species", "Clinical", "Correlation")
  melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
  
  # Create continuous variables plot
  p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Species, fill = Correlation)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                        limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Species Correlations with Clinical Variables -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Clinical Variables",
      y = "Species",
      fill = "Correlation"
    )
  
  # Prepare diabetes plot data
  diab_df <- data.frame(
    Species = species_cols,
    Effect = results$diab_effect,
    P_value = results$diab_p
  )
  diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05
  
  # Create diabetes plot
  p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Species, x = Effect)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "plum4") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Species Associations with Diabetes Status -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
      y = NULL,
      fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
    )
  
  list(continuous = p1, diabetes = p2)
}
```


```{r}
# Create plots for each ethnic group
plots_C <- create_plots(results_C, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Chinese")
plots_I <- create_plots(results_I, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Indian")
plots_M <- create_plots(results_M, species_cols, continuous_vars, "Malay")

# plots_C
# plots_I 
# plots_M
```


```{r}
# Arrange all plots
# Continuous variables plots on top row, diabetes plots on bottom row
grid.arrange(
  plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
  plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
  ncol = 3, nrow = 2
)


# ggsave("figure/ethnic_stratified_analysis.pdf",
#        arrangeGrob(plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
#                   plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
#                   ncol = 3, nrow = 2),
#        width = 24, height = 16)
```



```{r}
# Create summary table

diabetes_dist <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, diabetes_status) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  # Calculate percentages within each ethnic group
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count/sum(count) * 100)

diabetes_dist <- diabetes_dist %>%
  mutate(diabetes_status = factor(diabetes_status, levels = c("diabetes", "pre-diabetes", "normal")))
# Create a plot
ggplot(diabetes_dist, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = count, fill = diabetes_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Diabetes Status (based on Hba1C) by Ethnic Group",
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Diabetes Status"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# Print numerical summary
print(diabetes_dist)
```
```{r}
merged_data$glucose
diabetes_dist <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, glucose) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  # Calculate percentages within each ethnic group
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count/sum(count) * 100)

diabetes_dist <- diabetes_dist %>%
  mutate(glucose = factor(glucose, levels = c("diabetes", "pre-diabetes", "normal", "hypoglycemia")))

# Create a plot
ggplot(diabetes_dist, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = count, fill = glucose)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Diabetes Status (based on glucose) by Ethnic Group",
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Diabetes Status"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# Print numerical summary
print(diabetes_dist)
```
```{r}
species_filtered2
```


## overall analysis for clinical variables
```{r}
species_filtered2
# Match samples and create merged dataset
merged_data_a <- species_filtered2 %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")
species_cols2 = colnames(species_filtered2[,-1])
species_cols2
```


```{r}
# Assuming merged_data is already loaded and contains species and metadata columns
# Create OTU Table
merged_data_a$BMI_group <- ifelse(merged_data_a$BMI > 25, "Obese", "Non-Obese")
OTU <- otu_table(as.matrix(merged_data_a[, species_cols2]), taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
merged_data_a
# Make sure the row names of the OTU table are the Participant IDs
rownames(OTU) <- merged_data_a$Participant_ID

# Create Sample Data
# Ensure that the sample_data dataframe has Participant_ID as row names
samples_data <- sample_data(merged_data_a[, c("diabetes_status", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI","BMI_group")])
rownames(samples_data) <- merged_data_a$Participant_ID

# Create a phyloseq object
physeq <- phyloseq(OTU, samples_data)
OTU
```
##obese alpha beta
```{r}
comparisons <- list(c("Obese", "Non-Obese"))
a1 = plot_richness(physeq, x = "BMI_group", color = "BMI_group", measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson", "Chao1", "Fisher")) + geom_boxplot() + theme_bw()+
    stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisons, method = "t.test", label = "p.signif", symnum.args = list(cutpoints = c(0, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 1), symbols = c("***", "**", "*", "ns")))
ggsave(
  filename = "figure/figure6/obese_alpha.pdf",
  plot = a1,
  height = 6, 
  width = 9,
  device = cairo_pdf)

```
```{r}
# Compute alpha diversity measures for the physeq object
# alpha_div <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson", "Chao1", "Fisher"))
# 
# # Add BMI group information to the alpha diversity data
# alpha_div$BMI_group <- sam_data$BMI_group
# 
# # Run statistical tests for each diversity measure
# p_values <- data.frame(
#   Measure = c("Shannon", "Simpson", "Chao1", "Fisher"),
#   p_value = c(
#     t.test(alpha_div$Shannon ~ alpha_div$BMI_group)$p.value,
#     t.test(alpha_div$Simpson ~ alpha_div$BMI_group)$p.value,
#     t.test(alpha_div$Chao1 ~ alpha_div$BMI_group)$p.value,
#     t.test(alpha_div$Fisher ~ alpha_div$BMI_group)$p.value
#   )
# )
# 
# # Optionally, adjust p-values for multiple comparisons (e.g., using FDR correction)
# p_values$p_adj <- p.adjust(p_values$p_value, method = "fdr")
# print(p_values)
```



```{r}
dist = phyloseq::distance(physeq, method="bray")
phylo.beta = ordinate(physeq, method="PCoA", distance=dist)

b1 = plot_ordination(physeq, phylo.beta, type = "samples", color = "BMI_group") + theme_bw() + stat_ellipse(aes(group=BMI_group))
ggsave(
  filename = "figure/figure6/obese_beta.pdf",
  plot = b1,
  height = 6, 
  width = 9,
  device = cairo_pdf)
```
```{r}
library(vegan)
sample_data(physeq)$BMI_group <- factor(sample_data(physeq)$BMI_group)

physeq <- subset_samples(physeq, !is.na(sample_data(physeq)$BMI_group))

# Calculate the distance matrix using the ape package
dist <- phyloseq::distance(physeq, method = "bray")
dist_matrix <- as.matrix(dist)

# Perform PERMANOVA using adonis2
permanova <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ sample_data(physeq)$BMI_group)

# Print the PERMANOVA results
print(permanova)
```

##diabete alpha beta
```{r}
#comparisons <- list(c("Obese", "Non-Obese"))
physeq_f <- subset_samples(physeq, !is.na(sample_data(physeq)$diabetes_status))
a2 = plot_richness(physeq_f, x = "diabetes_status", color = "diabetes_status", measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson", "Chao1", "Fisher")) + geom_boxplot() + theme_bw()
ggsave(
  filename = "figure/figure6/diabete_alpha.pdf",
  plot = a2,
  height = 6, 
  width = 9,
  device = cairo_pdf)

```

```{r}
dist = phyloseq::distance(physeq_f, method="bray")
phylo.beta = ordinate(physeq_f, method="PCoA", distance=dist)

b2 = plot_ordination(physeq_f, phylo.beta, type = "samples", color = "diabetes_status") + theme_bw() + stat_ellipse(aes(group=diabetes_status))
ggsave(
  filename = "figure/figure6/diabetes_beta.pdf",
  plot = b2,
  height = 6, 
  width = 9,
  device = cairo_pdf)
```
```{r}
library(vegan)
sample_data(physeq_f)$diabetes_status <- factor(sample_data(physeq_f)$diabetes_status)

#physeq <- subset_samples(physeq, !is.na(sample_data(physeq)$BMI_group))

# Calculate the distance matrix using the ape package
dist <- phyloseq::distance(physeq_f, method = "bray")
dist_matrix <- as.matrix(dist)

# Perform PERMANOVA using adonis2
permanova <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ sample_data(physeq_f)$diabetes_status)

# Print the PERMANOVA results
print(permanova)
```


```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)

alpha_diversity <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Shannon")
# Convert rownames to a column for merging
alpha_diversity$Participant_ID <- rownames(alpha_diversity)

# Extract sample metadata from the phyloseq object and make it a regular data frame
sample_metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq))
# Ensure 'Participant_ID' is a column for merging
sample_metadata$Participant_ID <- rownames(sample_metadata)

# Merge alpha diversity data with sample metadata
alpha_diversity_full <- merge(alpha_diversity, sample_metadata, by = "Participant_ID")


# Now you can plot or analyze your merged data
# For example, to visualize alpha diversity across diabetes status:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(alpha_diversity_full, aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Shannon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Alpha Diversity across Diabetes Status", y = "Shannon Diversity Index", x = "Diabetes Status")

```

## food and clinical variables
```{r}
food_data
merged_data_f = food_data %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")
```

```{r}
food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
clinical_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "BMI", "HOMA_IR", "HOMA_B")

# Initialize matrices for correlations and p-values
cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))
p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(clinical_vars))

# Calculate partial correlations
for(i in 1:length(food_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(clinical_vars)) {
    # Create temporary dataset with ranked variables
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      food = rank(merged_data_f[[food_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data_f[[clinical_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data_f$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    # Calculate partial correlation
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$food, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    # Cap correlation values at ±0.2
    cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
}

# Add row and column names
rownames(cor_matrix) <- food_cols
colnames(cor_matrix) <- clinical_vars
rownames(p_matrix) <- food_cols
colnames(p_matrix) <- clinical_vars

# Perform clustering
food_dist <- dist(cor_matrix)
food_clust <- hclust(food_dist)
food_order <- food_clust$labels[food_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Convert to long format
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cor) <- c("Food", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cor$Significant <- melted_p$value < 0.05

# Set factor levels based on clustering
melted_cor$Food <- factor(melted_cor$Food, levels = food_order)
melted_cor$Clinical <- factor(melted_cor$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)

# Create the plot
ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Clinical, y = Food, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spearman Correlations between Food and Clinical Variables",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Food"
  )

```



```{r}


# Define columns
food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", "BMI", "HOMA_IR", "HOMA_B")

# Initialize matrices for continuous variables
cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))

# Initialize vectors for diabetes status
diab_effect <- numeric(length(food_cols))
diab_p <- numeric(length(food_cols))

# Calculate correlations for continuous variables
for(i in 1:length(food_cols)) {
  for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      food = rank(merged_data_f[[food_cols[i]]]),
      clinical = rank(merged_data_f[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
      gender = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
      age = rank(merged_data_f$FREG8_Age),
      ethnic_C = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C"),
      ethnic_I = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I"),
      ethnic_M = as.numeric(merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M")
    )
    
    pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$food, temp_data$clinical,
                            temp_data[,c("gender", "age", 
                                       "ethnic_C", "ethnic_I", "ethnic_M")])
    
    cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
    cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
  }
  
  # Calculate diabetes association
  temp_data <- data.frame(
    food_abundance = merged_data_f[[food_cols[i]]],
    gender = merged_data_f$FREG7_Gender,
    age = merged_data_f$FREG8_Age,
    ethnicity = merged_data_f$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
    diabetes = merged_data_f$diabetes_status
  )
  
  model <- lm(food_abundance ~ gender + age + ethnicity, data = temp_data)
  food_residuals <- residuals(model)
  
  kw_test <- kruskal.test(food_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
  
  groups <- split(food_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
  means <- sapply(groups, mean)
  overall_mean <- mean(food_residuals)
  ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
  ss_total <- sum((food_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
  eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
  
  if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
    direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
  } else {
    direction <- 1
  }
  
  diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
  diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
}

# Prepare continuous variables plot
rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- food_cols
colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- food_cols
colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars

# Clustering for continuous variables
food_dist <- dist(cont_cor_matrix)
food_clust <- hclust(food_dist)
food_order <- food_clust$labels[food_clust$order]

clinical_dist <- dist(t(cont_cor_matrix))
clinical_clust <- hclust(clinical_dist)
clinical_order <- clinical_clust$labels[clinical_clust$order]

# Prepare data for plots
# Continuous variables
melted_cont <- melt(cont_cor_matrix)
melted_cont_p <- melt(cont_p_matrix)
colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Food", "Clinical", "Correlation")
melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
melted_cont$Food <- factor(melted_cont$Food, levels = food_order)
melted_cont$Clinical <- factor(melted_cont$Clinical, levels = clinical_order)

# Diabetes status
diab_df <- data.frame(
  Food = food_cols,
  Effect = diab_effect,
  P_value = diab_p
)
diab_df$Food <- factor(diab_df$Food, levels = food_order)
diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05

# Create continuous variables plot
p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Food, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                      limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Food Correlations with Clinical Variables",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Clinical Variables",
    y = "Food",
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

# Create diabetes status plot
p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Food, x = Effect)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(brewer.pal(9, "PuBu"))) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "darkmagenta") +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
            color = "black", size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Food Associations with Diabetes Status",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender, Age, and Ethnicity\n* indicates p < 0.05",
    x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
  )

# Arrange plots side by side
grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1))

# Optionally save the combined plot
ggsave("figure/clinical_food_associations3.pdf", arrangeGrob(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.5, 1)), width = 15, height = 10)
```

## separate ethnic groups
```{r}

# Function to perform analysis for one ethnic group
analyze_ethnic_group <- function(merged_data_f, ethnic_group) {
  # Filter data for the specific ethnic group
  ethnic_data <- merged_data_f %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic_group)
  
  # Get columns
  food_cols <- colnames(food_data)[colnames(food_data) != "Participant_ID"]
  continuous_vars <- c("DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent", "DLAB83_CRP", 
                      "DLAB81_Insulin", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "BMI", "HOMA")
  
  # Initialize matrices
  cont_cor_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  cont_p_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(food_cols), ncol = length(continuous_vars))
  diab_effect <- numeric(length(food_cols))
  diab_p <- numeric(length(food_cols))
  
  # Calculate correlations for continuous variables
  for(i in 1:length(food_cols)) {
    for(j in 1:length(continuous_vars)) {
      temp_data <- data.frame(
        food = rank(ethnic_data[[food_cols[i]]]),
        clinical = rank(ethnic_data[[continuous_vars[j]]]),
        gender = as.numeric(ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender == "M"),
        age = rank(ethnic_data$FREG8_Age)
      )
      
      # Now adjusting only for gender and age
      pcor_result <- pcor.test(temp_data$food, temp_data$clinical,
                              temp_data[,c("gender", "age")])
      
      cont_cor_matrix[i,j] <- pmin(pmax(pcor_result$estimate, -0.15), 0.15)
      cont_p_matrix[i,j] <- pcor_result$p.value
    }
    
    # Calculate diabetes association
    temp_data <- data.frame(
      food_abundance = ethnic_data[[food_cols[i]]],
      gender = ethnic_data$FREG7_Gender,
      age = ethnic_data$FREG8_Age,
      diabetes = ethnic_data$diabetes_status
    )
    
    # Adjust for gender and age only
    model <- lm(food_abundance ~ gender + age, data = temp_data)
    food_residuals <- residuals(model)
    
    kw_test <- kruskal.test(food_residuals ~ temp_data$diabetes)
    
    groups <- split(food_residuals, temp_data$diabetes)
    means <- sapply(groups, mean)
    overall_mean <- mean(food_residuals)
    ss_between <- sum(sapply(groups, length) * (means - overall_mean)^2)
    ss_total <- sum((food_residuals - overall_mean)^2)
    eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
    
    if("diabetes" %in% names(means) && "normal" %in% names(means)) {
      direction <- sign(means["diabetes"] - means["normal"])
    } else {
      direction <- 1
    }
    
    diab_effect[i] <- direction * eta_squared
    diab_p[i] <- kw_test$p.value
  }
  
  # Prepare matrices
  rownames(cont_cor_matrix) <- food_cols
  colnames(cont_cor_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  rownames(cont_p_matrix) <- food_cols
  colnames(cont_p_matrix) <- continuous_vars
  
  # Return results
  list(
    cont_cor = cont_cor_matrix,
    cont_p = cont_p_matrix,
    diab_effect = diab_effect,
    diab_p = diab_p
  )
}

# Analyze each ethnic group
results_C <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data_f, "C")
results_I <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data_f, "I")
results_M <- analyze_ethnic_group(merged_data_f, "M")

# Function to create plots for one ethnic group
create_plots <- function(results, food_cols, continuous_vars, group_name) {
  # Prepare continuous variables plot
  melted_cont <- melt(results$cont_cor)
  melted_cont_p <- melt(results$cont_p)
  colnames(melted_cont) <- c("Food", "Clinical", "Correlation")
  melted_cont$Significant <- melted_cont_p$value < 0.05
  
  # Create continuous variables plot
  p1 <- ggplot(melted_cont, aes(x = Clinical, y = Food, fill = Correlation)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)), 
                        limits = c(-0.15, 0.15)) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", "")), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Food Correlations with Clinical Variables -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Clinical Variables",
      y = "Food",
      fill = "Correlation"
    )
  
  # Prepare diabetes plot data
  diab_df <- data.frame(
    Food = food_cols,
    Effect = results$diab_effect,
    P_value = results$diab_p
  )
  diab_df$Significant <- diab_df$P_value < 0.05
  
  # Create diabetes plot
  p2 <- ggplot(diab_df, aes(y = Food, x = Effect)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = abs(Effect))) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", high = "plum4") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Significant, "*", ""), x = 0), 
              color = "black", size = 5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Food Associations with Diabetes Status -", group_name),
      subtitle = "Adjusted for Gender and Age\n* indicates p < 0.05",
      x = "Effect Size (Eta Squared)",
      y = NULL,
      fill = "Absolute\nEffect Size"
    )
  
  list(continuous = p1, diabetes = p2)
}
```


```{r}
# Create plots for each ethnic group
plots_C <- create_plots(results_C, food_cols, continuous_vars, "Chinese")
plots_I <- create_plots(results_I, food_cols, continuous_vars, "Indian")
plots_M <- create_plots(results_M, food_cols, continuous_vars, "Malay")

plots_C
plots_I
plots_M
```


```{r}
# Arrange all plots
# Continuous variables plots on top row, diabetes plots on bottom row
grid.arrange(
  plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
  plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
  ncol = 3, nrow = 2
)


ggsave("figure/ethnic_food_clinical_analysis.pdf",
       arrangeGrob(plots_C$continuous, plots_I$continuous, plots_M$continuous,
                  plots_C$diabetes, plots_I$diabetes, plots_M$diabetes,
                  ncol = 3, nrow = 2),
       width = 24, height = 16)
```



## volcano diabete vs non-diabete
```{r}
threshold <- nrow(species1) * 0.05
species_filtered2 <- species1 %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)
species_filtered2

merged_data_filter = species_filtered2 %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

species_filtered2
```

```{r}
# Create a new diabetes status grouping
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(diabetes_group = case_when(
    diabetes_status == "diabetes" ~ "diabetes",
    diabetes_status %in% c("pre-diabetes", "normal") ~ "non_diabetes"
  ))

merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(obesity_status = case_when(
    BMI < 18.5 ~ "Underweight",
    BMI >= 18.5 & BMI < 23 ~ "Normal",
    BMI >= 23 & BMI < 25 ~ "Overweight",
    BMI >= 25 & BMI < 30 ~ "Obese Class I",
    BMI >= 30 ~ "Obese Class II",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # For any missing or invalid BMI values
  ))

# If you want a simpler classification, you can use this instead:
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(obesity_binary = case_when(
    BMI < 25 ~ "Non-obese",
    BMI >= 25 ~ "Obese",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
```


```{r}
# Get species columns (excluding Participant_ID)

# species_cols

#   t_test <- t.test(
#     get(species_name) ~ diabetes_group, 
#     data = diabetes_data
#   )

#   # Calculate mean values for each group
#   means <- diabetes_data %>%
#     group_by(diabetes_group) %>%
#     summarise(mean_val = mean(get(species_name))) %>%
#     pivot_wider(names_from = diabetes_group, values_from = mean_val)

#     species = species_name,
#     log2FoldChange = log2FC,
#     pvalue = t_test$p.value

# ggplot(volcano_data, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
#   geom_point(aes(color = abs(log2FoldChange) > 0.5 & padj < 0.1)) +
#   geom_text_repel(

#     aes(label = species),

#   geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed") +
#   scale_color_manual(values = c("grey", "red")) +
#   labs(
#     x = "log2 Fold Change (Diabetes vs Non-diabetes)",
#     y = "-log10 adjusted p-value",
#     title = "Volcano Plot of Species Abundance Changes in Diabetes vs Non-diabetes"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(legend.position = "none")
```
##GMHI
```{r}
# GMHI Calculation adapted for your data format
# Your data: species_filtered2 with Participant_ID in first column, absolute abundances

library(tidyverse)

# First, let's create the species lists adapted to your naming format
# (converting from s__Genus_species to "Genus species")

# Health-prevalent species (MH) - 7 species
MH_species_adapted <- c(
  "Alistipes senegalensis",
  "Bacteroidales bacterium ph8", 
  "Bifidobacterium adolescentis",
  "Bifidobacterium angulatum", 
  "Bifidobacterium catenulatum",
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 8 1 57FAA",  # Note: multiple underscores become spaces
  "Sutterella wadsworthensis"
)

# Health-scarce species (MN) - 43 species  
MN_species_adapted <- c(
  "Anaerotruncus colihominis",
  "Atopobium parvulum",
  "Bifidobacterium dentium",
  "Blautia producta", 
  "candidate division TM7 single cell isolate TM7c",
  "Clostridiales bacterium 1 7 47FAA",
  "Clostridium asparagiforme",
  "Clostridium bolteae",
  "Clostridium citroniae", 
  "Clostridium clostridioforme",
  "Clostridium hathewayi",
  "Clostridium nexile",
  "Clostridium ramosum",
  "Clostridium symbiosum",
  "Eggerthella lenta",
  "Erysipelotrichaceae bacterium 2 2 44A",
  "Flavonifractor plautii",
  "Fusobacterium nucleatum", 
  "Gemella morbillorum",
  "Gemella sanguinis",
  "Granulicatella adiacens",
  "Holdemania filiformis",
  "Klebsiella pneumoniae",
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 1 4 56FAA",
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 2 1 58FAA", 
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 3 1 57FAA CT1",
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 5 1 57FAA",
  "Lachnospiraceae bacterium 9 1 43BFAA",
  "Lactobacillus salivarius",
  "Peptostreptococcus stomatis",
  "Ruminococcaceae bacterium D16",
  "Ruminococcus gnavus", 
  "Solobacterium moorei",
  "Streptococcus anginosus",
  "Streptococcus australis", 
  "Streptococcus gordonii",
  "Streptococcus infantis",
  "Streptococcus mitis oralis pneumoniae", 
  "Streptococcus sanguinis",
  "Streptococcus vestibularis",
  "Subdoligranulum sp 4 3 54A2FAA",
  "Subdoligranulum variabile",
  "Veillonella atypica"
)

# Function to calculate GMHI
calculate_GMHI <- function(species_data) {
  
  # Step 0: Debug - check data structure
  print("Data structure:")
  print(str(species_data))
  print("First few columns:")
  print(head(species_data[, 1:min(5, ncol(species_data))]))
  
  # Step 1: Prepare data - set Participant_ID as rownames
  rownames(species_data) <- species_data$Participant_ID
  species_data <- species_data[, -1]  # Remove Participant_ID column
  
  # Step 1.5: Convert all columns to numeric (handle any character columns)
  # Check which columns are not numeric
  non_numeric_cols <- sapply(species_data, function(x) !is.numeric(x))
  if(any(non_numeric_cols)) {
    print("Non-numeric columns found:")
    print(names(species_data)[non_numeric_cols])
    print("Converting to numeric...")
    
    # Convert to numeric, coercing characters to NA, then NA to 0
    species_data[non_numeric_cols] <- lapply(species_data[non_numeric_cols], function(x) {
      numeric_x <- as.numeric(as.character(x))
      numeric_x[is.na(numeric_x)] <- 0
      return(numeric_x)
    })
  }
  
  # Check for any remaining issues
  print("Data summary after conversion:")
  print(summary(species_data[, 1:min(3, ncol(species_data))]))
  
  # Step 2: Convert to relative abundances (normalize by row sums)
  row_sums <- rowSums(species_data, na.rm = TRUE)
  print(paste("Row sums range:", min(row_sums), "to", max(row_sums)))
  
  # Avoid division by zero
  row_sums[row_sums == 0] <- 1
  species_profile <- sweep(species_data, 1, row_sums, `/`)
  
  # Step 3: Remove very low abundances and transpose for processing
  species_profile[species_profile < 0.00001] <- 0
  species_profile_t <- data.frame(t(species_profile), check.names = FALSE)
  
  # Step 4: Extract MH and MN species present in your data
  # Check which species names match (you may need to adjust these)
  available_species <- rownames(species_profile_t)
  
  # Find matching MH species
  MH_matches <- available_species[available_species %in% MH_species_adapted]
  MN_matches <- available_species[available_species %in% MN_species_adapted]
  
  print(paste("Found", length(MH_matches), "MH species out of 7"))
  print(paste("Found", length(MN_matches), "MN species out of 43"))
  if(length(MH_matches) > 0) {
    print(paste("MH species found:", paste(MH_matches, collapse = ", ")))
  } else {
    print("No MH species found in data")
  }
  if(length(MN_matches) > 0) {
    print(paste("MN species found:", paste(MN_matches, collapse = ", ")))
  } else {
    print("No MN species found in data")
  }
  
  # Extract the matching species data
  if(length(MH_matches) > 0) {
    MH_species_data <- species_profile_t[MH_matches, , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    MH_species_data <- data.frame(matrix(0, nrow = 1, ncol = ncol(species_profile_t)))
    colnames(MH_species_data) <- colnames(species_profile_t)
  }
  
  if(length(MN_matches) > 0) {
    MN_species_data <- species_profile_t[MN_matches, , drop = FALSE]  
  } else {
    MN_species_data <- data.frame(matrix(0, nrow = 1, ncol = ncol(species_profile_t)))
    colnames(MN_species_data) <- colnames(species_profile_t)
  }
  
  # Step 5: Calculate Shannon diversity
  alpha <- function(x) {
    x_pos <- x[x > 0]
    if(length(x_pos) == 0) return(0)
    sum(log(x_pos) * x_pos) * (-1)
  }
  
  MH_shannon <- apply(MH_species_data, 2, alpha)
  MN_shannon <- apply(MN_species_data, 2, alpha)
  
  # Step 6: Calculate richness
  R_MH <- apply(MH_species_data, 2, function(x) sum(x > 0))
  R_MN <- apply(MN_species_data, 2, function(x) sum(x > 0))
  
  # Step 7: Calculate collective abundances
  MH_prime <- 7   # Total possible MH species
  MN_prime <- 31  # Reference value from original study
  
  psi_MH <- (R_MH / MH_prime) * MH_shannon
  psi_MN <- (R_MN / MN_prime) * MN_shannon
  
  # Step 8: Calculate GMHI
  GMHI <- log10((psi_MH + 0.00001) / (psi_MN + 0.00001))
  
  # Return results with original participant IDs
  results <- data.frame(
    Participant_ID = rownames(species_data),  # This should preserve your original IDs
    GMHI = as.numeric(GMHI),
    R_MH = R_MH,
    R_MN = R_MN,
    MH_shannon = MH_shannon,
    MN_shannon = MN_shannon,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(results)
}

```


```{r}

# Calculate GMHI for your data
GMHI_results <- calculate_GMHI(species_filtered2)
GMHI_results$Participant_ID <- species_filtered2$Participant_ID
# View results
print(GMHI_results)

# Save results
#write.csv(GMHI_results, "GMHI_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
GMHI_results
```

```{r}
merged_data_filter_GMHI <- inner_join(GMHI_results, metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Step 2: Create diabetes status grouping
merged_data_filter_GMHI <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>%
  mutate(diabetes_group = case_when(
    diabetes_status == "diabetes" ~ "diabetes",
    diabetes_status %in% c("pre-diabetes", "normal") ~ "non_diabetes",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Handle any other values
  ))

# Step 3: Create detailed obesity status classification
merged_data_filter_GMHI <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>%
  mutate(obesity_status = case_when(
    BMI < 18.5 ~ "Underweight",
    BMI >= 18.5 & BMI < 23 ~ "Normal",
    BMI >= 23 & BMI < 25 ~ "Overweight", 
    BMI >= 25 & BMI < 30 ~ "Obese Class I",
    BMI >= 30 ~ "Obese Class II",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # For any missing or invalid BMI values
  ))

# Step 4: Create binary obesity classification
merged_data_filter_GMHI <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>%
  mutate(obesity_binary = case_when(
    BMI < 25 ~ "Non-obese",
    BMI >= 25 ~ "Obese", 
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
merged_data_filter_GMHI
```
### test gmhi with obesity
```{r}
obesity_summary <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>%
  filter(!is.na(obesity_binary) & !is.na(GMHI)) %>%
  group_by(obesity_binary) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_GMHI = round(mean(GMHI), 3),
    sd_GMHI = round(sd(GMHI), 3),
    median_GMHI = round(median(GMHI), 3),
    min_GMHI = round(min(GMHI), 3),
    max_GMHI = round(max(GMHI), 3),
    mean_BMI = round(mean(BMI, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
print(obesity_summary)

# Detailed obesity categories
print("\nGMHI by Detailed Obesity Categories:")
detailed_summary <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>%
  filter(!is.na(obesity_status) & !is.na(GMHI)) %>%
  group_by(obesity_status) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_GMHI = round(mean(GMHI), 3),
    sd_GMHI = round(sd(GMHI), 3),
    median_GMHI = round(median(GMHI), 3),
    mean_BMI = round(mean(BMI, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_BMI)
print(detailed_summary)
```
### t test
```{r}
valid_data <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>% 
  filter(!is.na(obesity_binary) & !is.na(GMHI))

if(nrow(valid_data) > 0 && length(unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)) == 2) {
  ttest_result <- t.test(GMHI ~ obesity_binary, data = valid_data)
  print("T-test (Non-obese vs Obese):")
  print(paste("Mean difference:", round(ttest_result$estimate[1] - ttest_result$estimate[2], 4)))
  print(paste("P-value:", round(ttest_result$p.value, 4)))
  print(paste("95% CI for difference:", round(ttest_result$conf.int[1], 4), "to", round(ttest_result$conf.int[2], 4)))
  
  # Effect size (Cohen's d)
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((sum(!is.na(valid_data$GMHI[valid_data$obesity_binary == unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)[1]])) - 1) * 
                     var(valid_data$GMHI[valid_data$obesity_binary == unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)[1]], na.rm = TRUE) +
                     (sum(!is.na(valid_data$GMHI[valid_data$obesity_binary == unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)[2]])) - 1) * 
                     var(valid_data$GMHI[valid_data$obesity_binary == unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)[2]], na.rm = TRUE)) /
                    (sum(!is.na(valid_data$GMHI)) - 2))
  cohens_d <- (ttest_result$estimate[1] - ttest_result$estimate[2]) / pooled_sd
  print(paste("Cohen's d (effect size):", round(cohens_d, 3)))
}

# ANOVA for detailed obesity categories
valid_detailed <- merged_data_filter_GMHI %>% 
  filter(!is.na(obesity_status) & !is.na(GMHI))

if(nrow(valid_detailed) > 0 && length(unique(valid_detailed$obesity_status)) > 2) {
  anova_result <- aov(GMHI ~ obesity_status, data = valid_detailed)
  print("\nANOVA (Detailed Obesity Categories):")
  print(summary(anova_result))
  
  # Post-hoc test if ANOVA is significant
  anova_p <- summary(anova_result)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
  if(anova_p < 0.05) {
    print("\nPost-hoc Tukey HSD test:")
    tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)
    print(tukey_result)
  }
}
```
```{r}
p2 <- ggplot(merged_data_filter_GMHI %>% filter(!is.na(obesity_binary)), 
             aes(x = obesity_binary, y = GMHI, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "white") +
  labs(title = "GMHI by Obesity Status (Binary)",
       x = "Obesity Status",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Health Index (GMHI)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon"))
p2

```
```{r}
p3 <- ggplot(merged_data_filter_GMHI %>% filter(!is.na(obesity_status)), 
             aes(x = reorder(obesity_status, BMI, FUN = median, na.rm = TRUE), 
                 y = GMHI, fill = obesity_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "white") +
  labs(title = "GMHI by Detailed Obesity Categories",
       x = "Obesity Category",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Health Index (GMHI)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = "Set3")
p3
```
### linear regression
```{r}
lm_model <- lm(GMHI ~ BMI, data = merged_data_filter_GMHI)
print("Linear Regression Summary (GMHI ~ BMI):")
print(summary(lm_model))

# Extract key metrics
r_squared <- summary(lm_model)$r.squared
adj_r_squared <- summary(lm_model)$adj.r.squared
print(paste("R-squared:", round(r_squared, 4)))
print(paste("Adjusted R-squared:", round(adj_r_squared, 4)))

```

## bifido in obese and in indian
```{r}
species_names <- colnames(species_filtered2)[-1]  # excluding Participant_ID

# Find Bifidobacterium species
bifido_species <- species_names[grepl("Bifidobacterium", species_names, ignore.case = TRUE)]
print(paste("Found", length(bifido_species), "Bifidobacterium species:"))
print(bifido_species)

if(length(bifido_species) == 0) {
  # Try alternative patterns
  bifido_species <- species_names[grepl("Bifido", species_names, ignore.case = TRUE)]
  print("Trying 'Bifido' pattern:")
  print(bifido_species)
}

# Step 2: Calculate total Bifidobacterium abundance for each participant
print("\n2. CALCULATING BIFIDOBACTERIUM ABUNDANCES")
print("----------------------------------------")

if(length(bifido_species) > 0) {
  # Extract Bifidobacterium data
  bifido_data <- species_filtered2 %>%
    dplyr::select(Participant_ID, all_of(bifido_species))
  
  # Calculate total Bifidobacterium abundance
  bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium <- rowSums(bifido_data[, -1], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate total abundance across all species for relative abundance
  total_abundance <- rowSums(species_filtered2[, -1], na.rm = TRUE)
  bifido_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium <- (bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium / total_abundance) * 100
  
  # Add individual species relative abundances
  for(species in bifido_species) {
    rel_col_name <- paste0("Rel_", gsub(" ", "_", species))
    bifido_data[[rel_col_name]] <- (species_filtered2[[species]] / total_abundance) * 100
  }
  
  print("Bifidobacterium abundance summary:")
  print(summary(bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium))
  print("Relative abundance (%) summary:")
  print(summary(bifido_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium))
  
} else {
  print("No Bifidobacterium species found! Please check species names.")
  print("First 20 species in your dataset:")
  print(head(species_names, 20))
  stop("Cannot proceed without Bifidobacterium data")
}
```
```{r}
if(length(bifido_species) > 0) {
  # Extract Bifidobacterium data
  bifido_data <- species_filtered2 %>%
    dplyr::select(Participant_ID, all_of(bifido_species))
  
  # Calculate total Bifidobacterium abundance
  bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium <- rowSums(bifido_data[, -1], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate total abundance across all species for relative abundance
  total_abundance <- rowSums(species_filtered2[, -1], na.rm = TRUE)
  bifido_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium <- (bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium / total_abundance) * 100
  
  # Add individual species relative abundances
  for(species in bifido_species) {
    rel_col_name <- paste0("Rel_", gsub(" ", "_", species))
    bifido_data[[rel_col_name]] <- (species_filtered2[[species]] / total_abundance) * 100
  }
  
  print("Bifidobacterium abundance summary:")
  print(summary(bifido_data$Total_Bifidobacterium))
  print("Relative abundance (%) summary:")
  print(summary(bifido_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium))
  
} else {
  print("No Bifidobacterium species found! Please check species names.")
  print("First 20 species in your dataset:")
  print(head(species_names, 20))
  stop("Cannot proceed without Bifidobacterium data")
}
bifido_data
```
```{r}
merged_data_filter_GMHI
```

```{r}
library(ggpubr)
bifido_merged <- inner_join(bifido_data, merged_data_filter_GMHI, by = "Participant_ID")
valid_data <- bifido_merged %>% 
  filter(!is.na(obesity_binary) & !is.na(Relative_Bifidobacterium))

print(paste("Valid data for analysis:", nrow(valid_data), "participants"))

# T-test for relative abundance
if(nrow(valid_data) > 0 && length(unique(valid_data$obesity_binary)) == 2) {
  ttest_rel <- t.test(Relative_Bifidobacterium ~ obesity_binary, data = valid_data)
  
  print("T-test Results:")
  print(paste("Non-obese mean:", round(ttest_rel$estimate[1], 3), "%"))
  print(paste("Obese mean:", round(ttest_rel$estimate[2], 3), "%"))
  print(paste("Difference:", round(ttest_rel$estimate[1] - ttest_rel$estimate[2], 3), "%"))
  print(paste("P-value:", round(ttest_rel$p.value, 6)))
  
  # Determine significance level for stars
  p_value <- ttest_rel$p.value
  if(p_value < 0.001) {
    sig_star <- "***"
    sig_text <- "p < 0.001"
  } else if(p_value < 0.01) {
    sig_star <- "**"
    sig_text <- paste("p =", round(p_value, 3))
  } else if(p_value < 0.05) {
    sig_star <- "*"
    sig_text <- paste("p =", round(p_value, 3))
  } else {
    sig_star <- "ns"
    sig_text <- paste("p =", round(p_value, 3))
  }
  
  print(paste("Significance:", sig_star, "-", sig_text))
} else {
  print("Cannot perform t-test - insufficient data or groups")
  sig_star <- "ns"
  sig_text <- "Cannot test"
  p_value <- 1
}

# Step 4: Create summary statistics for the plot
summary_stats <- valid_data %>%
  group_by(obesity_binary) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    median = round(median(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    q25 = quantile(Relative_Bifidobacterium, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75 = quantile(Relative_Bifidobacterium, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

print("\nSummary Statistics:")
print(summary_stats)

# Step 5: Create the box plot with significance stars
print("\nCreating Box Plot...")

# Calculate position for significance annotation
y_max <- max(valid_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE)
y_position <- y_max * 1.1

# Create the main box plot
p1 <- ggplot(valid_data, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "white", stroke = 1) +
  
  # Add significance annotation
  annotate("segment", x = 1, xend = 2, y = y_position, yend = y_position, color = "black") +
  annotate("segment", x = 1, xend = 1, y = y_position, yend = y_position * 0.95, color = "black") +
  annotate("segment", x = 2, xend = 2, y = y_position, yend = y_position * 0.95, color = "black") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = y_position * 1.05, label = sig_star, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  
  # Labels and styling
  labs(
    title = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance by Obesity Status",
    subtitle = paste("T-test:", sig_text, "| n =", nrow(valid_data)),
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)",
    caption = paste("* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001, ns = not significant")
  ) +
  
  # Theme and colors
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15)))  # Extra space on top for annotation

# Display the plot
print(p1)

# Step 6: Alternative plot with ggpubr for automatic significance
print("\nCreating Alternative Plot with ggpubr...")

# Using ggpubr for automatic significance testing
p2 <- ggboxplot(valid_data, 
                x = "obesity_binary", 
                y = "Relative_Bifidobacterium",
                fill = "obesity_binary",
                palette = c("lightblue", "salmon"),
                add = "jitter",
                add.params = list(alpha = 0.5)) +
  
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test", 
                     label = "p.signif",
                     label.y = y_position,
                     size = 6) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance by Obesity Status",
    subtitle = paste("Automatic significance testing | n =", nrow(valid_data)),
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(p2)
```
```{r}
valid_diabetes_data <- bifido_merged %>% 
  filter(!is.na(diabetes_status) & !is.na(Relative_Bifidobacterium) & 
         diabetes_status %in% c("pre-diabetes", "diabetes", "normal"))

print(paste("Valid data for diabetes analysis:", nrow(valid_diabetes_data), "participants"))
print("Distribution by diabetes status:")
print(table(valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status))

# Step 3: Statistical tests for diabetes groups
print("\nStatistical Tests:")
print("==================")

# ANOVA test for overall differences
if(nrow(valid_diabetes_data) > 0 && length(unique(valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status)) >= 2) {
  anova_result <- aov(Relative_Bifidobacterium ~ diabetes_status, data = valid_diabetes_data)
  anova_summary <- summary(anova_result)
  anova_p <- anova_summary[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
  
  print("ANOVA Results:")
  print(anova_summary)
  print(paste("Overall ANOVA p-value:", round(anova_p, 6)))
  
  # Determine overall significance
  if(anova_p < 0.001) {
    overall_sig <- "***"
  } else if(anova_p < 0.01) {
    overall_sig <- "**"
  } else if(anova_p < 0.05) {
    overall_sig <- "*"
  } else {
    overall_sig <- "ns"
  }
  
  print(paste("Overall significance:", overall_sig))
  
  # Post-hoc pairwise comparisons if ANOVA is significant
  if(anova_p < 0.05) {
    print("\nPost-hoc Pairwise Comparisons (Tukey HSD):")
    tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)
    print(tukey_result)
    
    # Extract pairwise p-values for plotting
    pairwise_p <- tukey_result$diabetes_status[, "p adj"]
    pairwise_comparisons <- data.frame(
      comparison = names(pairwise_p),
      p_value = pairwise_p,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    # Add significance stars for each comparison
    pairwise_comparisons$sig_star <- sapply(pairwise_comparisons$p_value, function(p) {
      if(p < 0.001) "***"
      else if(p < 0.01) "**" 
      else if(p < 0.05) "*"
      else "ns"
    })
    
    print("Pairwise significance:")
    print(pairwise_comparisons)
  } else {
    pairwise_comparisons <- NULL
  }
} else {
  print("Cannot perform ANOVA - insufficient data or groups")
  overall_sig <- "ns"
  anova_p <- 1
  pairwise_comparisons <- NULL
}

# Step 4: Summary statistics by diabetes status
summary_diabetes <- valid_diabetes_data %>%
  group_by(diabetes_status) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    median = round(median(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    q25 = round(quantile(Relative_Bifidobacterium, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    q75 = round(quantile(Relative_Bifidobacterium, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(diabetes_status, c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes")))

print("\nSummary Statistics by Diabetes Status:")
print(summary_diabetes)

# Step 5: Create the main box plot
print("\nCreating Box Plot...")

# Order the diabetes status for logical progression
valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status <- factor(valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status, 
                                             levels = c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes"))

# Calculate y-position for significance annotations
y_max <- max(valid_diabetes_data$Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE)
y_positions <- c(y_max * 1.1, y_max * 1.2, y_max * 1.3)

# Create the main box plot
p1 <- ggplot(valid_diabetes_data, aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = diabetes_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "white", stroke = 1) +
  
  # Labels and styling
  labs(
    title = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance by Diabetes Status",
    subtitle = paste("ANOVA:", overall_sig, "| p =", round(anova_p, 4), "| n =", nrow(valid_diabetes_data)),
    x = "Diabetes Status",
    y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)",
    caption = "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001, ns = not significant"
  ) +
  
  # Theme and colors
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("normal" = "lightgreen", "pre-diabetes" = "orange", "diabetes" = "red")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("normal" = "Normal", "pre-diabetes" = "Pre-diabetes", "diabetes" = "Diabetes")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.2)))  # Extra space on top for annotations

# Add pairwise significance annotations if available
if(!is.null(pairwise_comparisons) && nrow(pairwise_comparisons) > 0) {
  # Add significance bars for significant comparisons
  sig_comparisons <- pairwise_comparisons[pairwise_comparisons$sig_star != "ns", ]
  
  if(nrow(sig_comparisons) > 0) {
    for(i in 1:nrow(sig_comparisons)) {
      comp <- sig_comparisons$comparison[i]
      sig <- sig_comparisons$sig_star[i]
      
      # Parse comparison (e.g., "diabetes-normal")
      groups <- unlist(strsplit(comp, "-"))
      group1 <- groups[1]
      group2 <- groups[2]
      
      # Get x positions
      x1 <- which(levels(valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status) == group1)
      x2 <- which(levels(valid_diabetes_data$diabetes_status) == group2)
      
      y_pos <- y_positions[i]
      
      # Add significance annotation
      p1 <- p1 + 
        annotate("segment", x = x1, xend = x2, y = y_pos, yend = y_pos, color = "black") +
        annotate("segment", x = x1, xend = x1, y = y_pos, yend = y_pos * 0.98, color = "black") +
        annotate("segment", x = x2, xend = x2, y = y_pos, yend = y_pos * 0.98, color = "black") +
        annotate("text", x = (x1 + x2)/2, y = y_pos * 1.02, label = sig, size = 5, fontface = "bold")
    }
  }
}

print(p1)
```


### combine
```{r}
library(gridExtra)  # For combining plots
library(grid)
valid_ethnic_data <- bifido_merged %>%
  filter(!is.na(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) & 
         !is.na(Relative_Bifidobacterium) &
         FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M"))

print(paste("Participants with valid ethnic and Bifidobacterium data:", nrow(valid_ethnic_data)))

# Check sample sizes by ethnic group
ethnic_counts <- table(valid_ethnic_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)
print("Sample sizes by ethnic group:")
print(ethnic_counts)

# Step 2: Create ethnic group labels
ethnic_labels <- c("C" = "Chinese", "I" = "Indian", "M" = "Malay")
valid_ethnic_data$Ethnic_Group_Label <- ethnic_labels[valid_ethnic_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group]

# ===============================================
# DIABETES ANALYSIS BY ETHNICITY
# ===============================================

# Filter for diabetes analysis
diabetes_ethnic_data <- valid_ethnic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(diabetes_status) & 
         diabetes_status %in% c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes"))

print(paste("Participants for diabetes analysis:", nrow(diabetes_ethnic_data)))

# Sample sizes by ethnic group and diabetes status
print("Sample sizes by ethnic group and diabetes status:")
diabetes_ethnic_table <- table(diabetes_ethnic_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                              diabetes_ethnic_data$diabetes_status)
print(diabetes_ethnic_table)

# Summary statistics by ethnicity and diabetes
diabetes_ethnic_summary <- diabetes_ethnic_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, Ethnic_Group_Label, diabetes_status) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    median = round(median(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, diabetes_status)

print("Summary statistics:")
print(diabetes_ethnic_summary)

# Statistical tests within each ethnic group
print("\nStatistical tests within each ethnic group (ANOVA):")
for(ethnic in c("C", "I", "M")) {
  ethnic_subset <- diabetes_ethnic_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic)
  
  if(nrow(ethnic_subset) > 0 && length(unique(ethnic_subset$diabetes_status)) >= 2) {
    anova_ethnic <- aov(Relative_Bifidobacterium ~ diabetes_status, data = ethnic_subset)
    anova_p <- summary(anova_ethnic)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    
    print(paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic], "- ANOVA p-value:", round(anova_p, 4)))
    
    if(anova_p < 0.05 && length(unique(ethnic_subset$diabetes_status)) > 2) {
      tukey_ethnic <- TukeyHSD(anova_ethnic)
      print(paste("  Significant differences found - Tukey HSD:"))
      print(tukey_ethnic$diabetes_status)
    }
  } else {
    print(paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic], "- Insufficient data for ANOVA"))
  }
}

# Create diabetes box plots by ethnicity with significance lines
diabetes_ethnic_data$diabetes_status <- factor(diabetes_ethnic_data$diabetes_status,
                                              levels = c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes"))

# Create separate plots for each ethnicity to add significance properly
diabetes_plots <- list()

for(ethnic in c("C", "I", "M")) {
  ethnic_subset <- diabetes_ethnic_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic)
  
  if(nrow(ethnic_subset) > 0) {
    # Perform statistical tests for this ethnicity
    comparisons_list <- list(c("normal", "pre-diabetes"), 
                           c("normal", "diabetes"), 
                           c("pre-diabetes", "diabetes"))
    
    # Filter comparisons to only include groups that exist
    available_groups <- unique(ethnic_subset$diabetes_status)
    valid_comparisons <- comparisons_list[sapply(comparisons_list, function(x) all(x %in% available_groups))]
    
    if(length(valid_comparisons) > 0 && length(available_groups) >= 2) {
      p_ethnic <- ggboxplot(ethnic_subset,
                           x = "diabetes_status", 
                           y = "Relative_Bifidobacterium",
                           fill = "diabetes_status",
                           palette = c("lightgreen", "orange", "red"),
                           add = "jitter",
                           add.params = list(alpha = 0.5, size = 1)) +
        
        stat_compare_means(comparisons = valid_comparisons,
                          method = "t.test",
                          label = "p.signif",
                          step.increase = 0.1,
                          tip.length = 0.02) +
        
        labs(
          title = paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic]),
          x = "Diabetes Status",
          y = if(ethnic == "C") "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)" else ""
        ) +
        
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "none"
        ) +
        scale_x_discrete(labels = c("normal" = "Normal", "pre-diabetes" = "Pre-diabetes", "diabetes" = "Diabetes"))
      
    } else {
      # Create basic plot without significance if insufficient groups
      p_ethnic <- ggplot(ethnic_subset, aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = diabetes_status)) +
        geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
        labs(
          title = paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic]),
          x = "Diabetes Status",
          y = if(ethnic == "C") "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)" else ""
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "none"
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c("normal" = "lightgreen", "pre-diabetes" = "orange", "diabetes" = "red")) +
        scale_x_discrete(labels = c("normal" = "Normal", "pre-diabetes" = "Pre-diabetes", "diabetes" = "Diabetes"))
    }
    
    diabetes_plots[[ethnic]] <- p_ethnic
  }
}

# Combine plots
if(length(diabetes_plots) > 0) {
  p_diabetes_ethnic <- grid.arrange(grobs = diabetes_plots, ncol = 3,
                                   top = textGrob("Bifidobacterium by Diabetes Status - Stratified by Ethnicity", 
                                                 gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")))
} else {
  # Fallback to original plot style
  p_diabetes_ethnic <- ggplot(diabetes_ethnic_data, 
                             aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = diabetes_status)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~Ethnic_Group_Label, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      title = "Bifidobacterium by Diabetes Status - Stratified by Ethnicity",
      x = "Diabetes Status",
      y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("normal" = "lightgreen", "pre-diabetes" = "orange", "diabetes" = "red")) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("normal" = "Normal", "pre-diabetes" = "Pre-diabetes", "diabetes" = "Diabetes"))
}

print(p_diabetes_ethnic)

# ===============================================
# OBESITY ANALYSIS BY ETHNICITY
# ===============================================



# Filter for obesity analysis
obesity_ethnic_data <- valid_ethnic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(obesity_binary))

print(paste("Participants for obesity analysis:", nrow(obesity_ethnic_data)))

# Sample sizes by ethnic group and obesity status
print("Sample sizes by ethnic group and obesity status:")
obesity_ethnic_table <- table(obesity_ethnic_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                             obesity_ethnic_data$obesity_binary)
print(obesity_ethnic_table)

# Summary statistics by ethnicity and obesity
obesity_ethnic_summary <- obesity_ethnic_data %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, Ethnic_Group_Label, obesity_binary) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    median = round(median(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, obesity_binary)

print("Summary statistics:")
print(obesity_ethnic_summary)

# Statistical tests within each ethnic group (t-tests)
print("\nStatistical tests within each ethnic group (t-tests):")
ttest_results <- data.frame()

for(ethnic in c("C", "I", "M")) {
  ethnic_subset <- obesity_ethnic_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic)
  
  if(nrow(ethnic_subset) > 0 && length(unique(ethnic_subset$obesity_binary)) == 2) {
    ttest_ethnic <- t.test(Relative_Bifidobacterium ~ obesity_binary, data = ethnic_subset)
    
    # Determine significance
    p_val <- ttest_ethnic$p.value
    if(p_val < 0.001) sig_star <- "***"
    else if(p_val < 0.01) sig_star <- "**"
    else if(p_val < 0.05) sig_star <- "*"
    else sig_star <- "ns"
    
    print(paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic], ":"))
    print(paste("  Non-obese mean:", round(ttest_ethnic$estimate[1], 3)))
    print(paste("  Obese mean:", round(ttest_ethnic$estimate[2], 3)))
    print(paste("  Difference:", round(ttest_ethnic$estimate[1] - ttest_ethnic$estimate[2], 3)))
    print(paste("  P-value:", round(p_val, 4), "(", sig_star, ")"))
    
    # Store results for plotting
    ttest_results <- rbind(ttest_results, data.frame(
      Ethnic_Group = ethnic,
      Ethnic_Label = ethnic_labels[ethnic],
      p_value = p_val,
      sig_star = sig_star,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  } else {
    print(paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic], "- Insufficient data for t-test"))
  }
}

# Create obesity box plots by ethnicity with significance lines
obesity_plots <- list()

for(ethnic in c("C", "I", "M")) {
  ethnic_subset <- obesity_ethnic_data %>% 
    filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic)
  
  if(nrow(ethnic_subset) > 0 && length(unique(ethnic_subset$obesity_binary)) == 2) {
    # Get significance result for this ethnicity
    ttest_result <- ttest_results %>% filter(Ethnic_Group == ethnic)
    
    if(nrow(ttest_result) > 0) {
      p_ethnic <- ggboxplot(ethnic_subset,
                           x = "obesity_binary", 
                           y = "Relative_Bifidobacterium",
```
