```{r}
                           fill = "obesity_binary",
                           palette = c("lightblue", "salmon"),
                           add = "jitter",
                           add.params = list(alpha = 0.5, size = 1)) +
        
        stat_compare_means(method = "t.test",
                          label = "p.signif",
                          comparisons = list(c("Non-obese", "Obese")),
                          tip.length = 0.02) +
        
        labs(
          title = paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic]),
          x = "Obesity Status",
          y = if(ethnic == "C") "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)" else ""
        ) +
        
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "none"
        )
      
    } else {
      # Create basic plot without significance
      p_ethnic <- ggplot(ethnic_subset, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = obesity_binary)) +
        geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
        labs(
          title = paste(ethnic_labels[ethnic]),
          x = "Obesity Status",
          y = if(ethnic == "C") "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)" else ""
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "none"
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon"))
    }
    
    obesity_plots[[ethnic]] <- p_ethnic
  }
}

# Combine obesity plots
if(length(obesity_plots) > 0) {
  p_obesity_ethnic <- grid.arrange(grobs = obesity_plots, ncol = 3,
                                  top = textGrob("Bifidobacterium by Obesity Status - Stratified by Ethnicity", 
                                                gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")))
} else {
  # Fallback plot
  p_obesity_ethnic <- ggplot(obesity_ethnic_data, 
                            aes(x = obesity_binary, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, fill = obesity_binary)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~Ethnic_Group_Label, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      title = "Bifidobacterium by Obesity Status - Stratified by Ethnicity",
      x = "Obesity Status", 
      y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon"))
}

print(p_obesity_ethnic)

# ===============================================
# COMBINED ANALYSIS
# ===============================================



# Create combined dataset with both diabetes and obesity info
combined_data <- valid_ethnic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(diabetes_status) & diabetes_status %in% c("normal", "pre-diabetes", "diabetes") &
         !is.na(obesity_binary))

print(paste("Participants with both diabetes and obesity data:", nrow(combined_data)))

# Create interaction plots
print("\nCreating interaction plots...")

# 1. Diabetes by ethnicity with obesity as color
p_diabetes_obesity <- ggplot(combined_data, 
                            aes(x = diabetes_status, y = Relative_Bifidobacterium, 
                                fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, position = position_dodge(0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~Ethnic_Group_Label) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Bifidobacterium by Diabetes Status and Obesity - Stratified by Ethnicity",
    x = "Diabetes Status",
    y = "Bifidobacterium Relative Abundance (%)",
    fill = "Obesity Status"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("normal" = "Normal", "pre-diabetes" = "Pre-diabetes", "diabetes" = "Diabetes"))

print(p_diabetes_obesity)

# 2. Statistical summary table for all combinations
print("\nComprehensive summary table:")
comprehensive_summary <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(Ethnic_Group_Label, diabetes_status, obesity_binary) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(Relative_Bifidobacterium, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(Ethnic_Group_Label, diabetes_status, obesity_binary)

print(comprehensive_summary)
```
## each bifido
```{r}
bifido_species_cols <- colnames(species_filtered2)[grepl("Bifidobacterium", colnames(species_filtered2), ignore.case = TRUE)]
bifido_species_cols
```
```{r}
species_long <- valid_ethnic_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, obesity_binary, all_of(bifido_species_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(bifido_species_cols),
               names_to = "Species",
               values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(
    Species = gsub("_", " ", Species),  # Replace underscores with spaces
    Species = str_to_title(Species)     # Capitalize first letter of each word
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(obesity_binary))  # Remove any missing obesity data




species_test_results <- data.frame()

for(species in unique(species_long$Species)) {
  species_data <- species_long %>% filter(Species == species)
  
  if(nrow(species_data) > 0 && length(unique(species_data$obesity_binary)) == 2) {
    # Perform t-test
    ttest_result <- t.test(Abundance ~ obesity_binary, data = species_data)
    
    # Calculate summary statistics
    summary_stats <- species_data %>%
      group_by(obesity_binary) %>%
      summarise(
        mean = mean(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
        median = median(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
        n = n(),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    
    # Determine significance
    p_val <- ttest_result$p.value
    if(p_val < 0.001) sig_star <- "***"
    else if(p_val < 0.01) sig_star <- "**"
    else if(p_val < 0.05) sig_star <- "*"
    else sig_star <- "ns"
    
    # Store results
    non_obese_mean <- ifelse(nrow(summary_stats %>% filter(obesity_binary == "Non-obese")) > 0,
                            summary_stats$mean[summary_stats$obesity_binary == "Non-obese"], NA)
    obese_mean <- ifelse(nrow(summary_stats %>% filter(obesity_binary == "Obese")) > 0,
                        summary_stats$mean[summary_stats$obesity_binary == "Obese"], NA)
    
    species_test_results <- rbind(species_test_results, data.frame(
      Species = species,
      Non_obese_mean = non_obese_mean,
      Obese_mean = obese_mean,
      Difference = ttest_result$estimate[1] - ttest_result$estimate[2],
      p_value = p_val,
      sig_star = sig_star,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
    
    print(paste(species, ":"))
    print(paste("  Non-obese mean:", round(ttest_result$estimate[1], 2)))
    print(paste("  Obese mean:", round(ttest_result$estimate[2], 2)))
    print(paste("  Difference:", round(ttest_result$estimate[1] - ttest_result$estimate[2], 2)))
    print(paste("  P-value:", round(p_val, 6), "(", sig_star, ")"))
  }
}

print("\nSpecies test results summary:")
print(species_test_results)
write.csv(species_test_results, "figure/bifido.csv")
# Step 5: Create stacked box plot
print("\nCreating stacked box plot...")

# Order species by mean abundance for better visualization
species_order <- species_long %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(mean_abundance = mean(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_abundance)) %>%
  pull(Species)

species_long$Species <- factor(species_long$Species, levels = species_order)

# Create the main stacked box plot
p_species_stacked <- ggplot(species_long, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, position = position_dodge(0.8), outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Add individual points
  geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.8, jitter.width = 0.2), 
             alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Individual Bifidobacterium Species Abundance by Obesity Status",
    x = "Bifidobacterium Species",
    y = "Absolute Abundance (counts)",
    fill = "Obesity Status"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "lightblue", "Obese" = "salmon")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15)))  # Extra space for significance annotations

# Add significance annotations
if(nrow(species_test_results) > 0) {
  for(i in 1:nrow(species_test_results)) {
    species_name <- species_test_results$Species[i]
    sig_star <- species_test_results$sig_star[i]
    
    if(sig_star != "ns") {
      # Get max y value for this species
      species_max_y <- max(species_long$Abundance[species_long$Species == species_name], na.rm = TRUE)
      y_position <- species_max_y * 1.1
      
      # Get x position (species index)
      x_position <- which(levels(species_long$Species) == species_name)
      
      # Add significance annotation
      p_species_stacked <- p_species_stacked +
        annotate("segment", x = x_position - 0.2, xend = x_position + 0.2, 
                y = y_position, yend = y_position, color = "black", size = 0.5) +
        annotate("segment", x = x_position - 0.2, xend = x_position - 0.2, 
                y = y_position, yend = y_position * 0.98, color = "black", size = 0.5) +
        annotate("segment", x = x_position + 0.2, xend = x_position + 0.2, 
                y = y_position, yend = y_position * 0.98, color = "black", size = 0.5) +
        annotate("text", x = x_position, y = y_position * 1.03, 
                label = sig_star, size = 4, fontface = "bold")
    }
  }
}

print(p_species_stacked)

```



## literature species
```{r}
diabetes_enriched <- c(
  "Lactobacillus gasseri",
  "Streptococcus mutans",
  "Clostridium citroniae",
  "Clostridium bolteae",
  "Escherichia coli",
  "Bacteroides fragilis",
  "Streptococcus salivarius",  # Example Streptococcus species, you may need to adjust based on your data
  "Streptococcus parasanguinis"
  # Add more species from literature
)

diabetes_depleted <- c(
  "Roseburia intestinalis",
  "Faecalibacterium prausnitzii",
  "Butyrivibrio crossotus",
  "Coprococcus eutactus", 
  "Turicibacter sanguinis",
  "Bacteroides plebeius",
  "Ruminococcus lactaris"
  # Add more species from literature
)

obesity_enriched <- c(
  "Ruminococcus gnavus",
  "Lactobacillus reuteri",
  "Lactobacillus gasseri"
  # Add more species from literature
)

obesity_depleted <- c(
  "Akkermansia muciniphila",
  "Lactobacillus paracasei",
  "Methanobacteriales smithii",
  "Bifidobacterium animalis"
  # Add more species from literature
)
# Function to analyze trends based on literature species
analyze_literature_species_trends <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, expected_direction) {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if (length(existing_species) == 0) {
    message("No matching species found in the dataset for this analysis.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Analyze each species
  results <- map_dfr(existing_species, function(species) {
    # Calculate mean values for each group
    means <- condition_data %>%
      group_by(get(condition_column)) %>%
      summarise(mean_val = mean(.data[[species]], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = `get(condition_column)`, values_from = mean_val)
    
    # Calculate log2 fold change
    log2FC <- log2(means[[2]] / means[[1]])
    
    # Check if the trend matches the expected direction
    trend_matches <- if (expected_direction == "enriched") {
      log2FC > 0
    } else {
      log2FC < 0
    }
    
    data.frame(
      species = species,
      log2FoldChange = log2FC,
      matches_literature = trend_matches
    )
  })
  
  return(results)
}

# Analyze diabetes-associated species trends
diabetes_enriched_trends <- analyze_literature_species_trends(
  diabetes_enriched,
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  "enriched"
)

diabetes_depleted_trends <- analyze_literature_species_trends(
  diabetes_depleted,
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  "depleted"
)

# Analyze obesity-associated species trends
obesity_enriched_trends <- analyze_literature_species_trends(
  obesity_enriched,
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  "enriched"
)

obesity_depleted_trends <- analyze_literature_species_trends(
  obesity_depleted,
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  "depleted"
)

# Combine and summarize results
create_summary_table <- function(trends, condition) {
  if (is.null(trends) || nrow(trends) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  trends %>%
    mutate(condition = condition) %>%
    dplyr::select(species, log2FoldChange, matches_literature, condition)
}

diabetes_enriched_summary <- create_summary_table(diabetes_enriched_trends, "Diabetes Enriched")
diabetes_depleted_summary <- create_summary_table(diabetes_depleted_trends, "Diabetes Depleted")
obesity_enriched_summary <- create_summary_table(obesity_enriched_trends, "Obesity Enriched")
obesity_depleted_summary <- create_summary_table(obesity_depleted_trends, "Obesity Depleted")

# Combine summaries into a single table
all_summaries <- bind_rows(
  diabetes_enriched_summary,
  diabetes_depleted_summary,
  obesity_enriched_summary,
  obesity_depleted_summary
)

# View the summary table
library(knitr)
kable(all_summaries, caption = "Summary of Literature Trends in Microbiome Data")

```

## microbiome score of obesity
## dysbiosis score
```{r}
# Select species present in your dataset
obesity_enriched_present <- intersect(obesity_enriched, colnames(merged_data_filter))
obesity_depleted_present <- intersect(obesity_depleted, colnames(merged_data_filter))

# Compute row-wise sums of enriched and depleted species
enriched_sum <- rowSums(merged_data_filter[, obesity_enriched_present], na.rm = TRUE)
depleted_sum <- rowSums(merged_data_filter[, obesity_depleted_present], na.rm = TRUE)

# Calculate dysbiosis score with pseudocount to avoid division by 0
merged_data_filter$dysbiosis_score <- log((enriched_sum + 1e-6) / (depleted_sum + 1e-6))

```
## imflamatrory score
```{r}
# Calculate inflammatory score for each participant

# Define inflammatory weights for each food category
# Positive values = pro-inflammatory
# Negative values = anti-inflammatory
inflammatory_weights <- c(
  Fish = -0.5,             # Anti-inflammatory (omega-3)
  Crustacean = 0.1,        # Slightly pro-inflammatory
  Mollusk = 0.1,           # Slightly pro-inflammatory
  Poultry = 0.2,           # Slightly pro-inflammatory
  Red_meat = 0.8,          # Pro-inflammatory
  Processed_meat = 1.0,    # Strongly pro-inflammatory
  Dairy = 0.3,             # Moderately pro-inflammatory for some people
  Cruciferous_veg = -0.7,  # Strongly anti-inflammatory
  Legumes = -0.6,          # Anti-inflammatory
  Mushrooms = -0.4,        # Anti-inflammatory
  Fruits = -0.6,           # Anti-inflammatory
  Papaya = -0.8,           # Strongly anti-inflammatory
  Orange = -0.7,           # Anti-inflammatory (vitamin C)
  Wholegrain = -0.5,       # Anti-inflammatory
  Chapati = -0.3,          # Slightly anti-inflammatory (depends on grain)
  Idli_Thosai = -0.2,      # Slightly anti-inflammatory (fermented)
  Noodle_dishes = 0.4,     # Moderately pro-inflammatory (refined carbs)
  Deep_fried = 0.9,        # Strongly pro-inflammatory
  Coffee = -0.3,           # Slightly anti-inflammatory
  Tea = -0.4,              # Anti-inflammatory (polyphenols)
  Lontong = 0.1,           # Slightly pro-inflammatory
  Pork = 0.7,              # Pro-inflammatory
  Rice = 0.2               # Slightly pro-inflammatory (refined carbs)
)

# Calculate inflammatory score for each participant
food_data$inflammatory_score <- 0

for (food in names(inflammatory_weights)) {
  if (food %in% colnames(food_data)) {
    food_data$inflammatory_score <- food_data$inflammatory_score + 
      food_data[[food]] * inflammatory_weights[food]
  }
}


# Summary statistics
summary(food_data$inflammatory_score)
#merged_data_filter
```
```{r}
metadata
```

## combine 3 scores
```{r}
combined_data <- merge(food_data, 
                       merged_data_filter[, c("Participant_ID", "dysbiosis_score", "obesity_binary", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "FREG7_Gender","diabetes_status")],
                       by = "Participant_ID", 
                       all.x = FALSE,  # Keep only matching participants
                       all.y = FALSE)

# Check the structure of the combined data
str(combined_data)

# Basic summary statistics
summary(combined_data[, c("inflammatory_score", "dysbiosis_score", "obesity_binary")])

# Examine correlations between inflammatory score and dysbiosis score
correlation <- cor.test(combined_data$inflammatory_score, combined_data$dysbiosis_score)
print(correlation)

# Compare inflammatory scores between obese and non-obese individuals
# Convert obesity_binary to factor if it isn't already
combined_data$obesity_binary <- as.factor(combined_data$obesity_binary)

# T-test to compare inflammatory scores by obesity status
t_test_result <- t.test(inflammatory_score ~ obesity_binary, data = combined_data)
print(t_test_result)

# Visualize the relationships
# Load ggplot2 for better visualizations
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Scatterplot of inflammatory score vs dysbiosis score, colored by obesity status
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = inflammatory_score, y = dysbiosis_score, color = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Relationship between Inflammatory Score and Dysbiosis Score",
       x = "Dietary Inflammatory Score",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis Score",
       color = "Obesity Status") +
  theme_minimal()

# 2. Boxplots comparing scores by obesity status
# For inflammatory score
p1 <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = inflammatory_score, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Inflammatory Score by Obesity Status",
       x = "Obesity Status",
       y = "Dietary Inflammatory Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# For dysbiosis score
p2 <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = dysbiosis_score, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Dysbiosis Score by Obesity Status",
       x = "Obesity Status",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)  # For non-overlapping text labels
library(viridis)  # For colorblind-friendly palettes

# Create an integrated visualization showing relationships between all three variables
# This creates a scatter plot with inflammatory score vs dysbiosis score
# with point size representing obesity status and color representing the combined risk

# First, let's calculate a combined score to use for coloring
# Standardize both scores to put them on the same scale
combined_data$inflammatory_z <- scale(combined_data$inflammatory_score)
combined_data$dysbiosis_z <- scale(combined_data$dysbiosis_score)

# Calculate a combined risk score (sum of z-scores)
combined_data$combined_risk <- combined_data$inflammatory_z + combined_data$dysbiosis_z

# Create a factor for obesity (for better labeling)
combined_data$obesity_status <- ifelse(combined_data$obesity_binary == 1, "Obese", "Non-obese")

# Create the integrated plot
integrated_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, 
                         aes(x = inflammatory_score, 
                             y = dysbiosis_score, 
                             size = obesity_status,
                             color = combined_risk)) +
  # Add points
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add contour lines to show density (optional)
  stat_density_2d(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray30", alpha = 0.5) +
  
  # Add correlation line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # Use a color scale that transitions from blue (low risk) to red (high risk)
  scale_color_viridis(option = "plasma", name = "Combined Risk\n(Inflammatory + Dysbiosis)") +
  
  # Set point sizes for obesity status
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = 2, "Obese" = 4), name = "Obesity Status") +
  
  # Add quadrant lines at median values
  geom_hline(yintercept = median(combined_data$dysbiosis_score), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(combined_data$inflammatory_score), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add quadrant labels
  annotate("text", 
           x = c(min(combined_data$inflammatory_score) + 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$inflammatory_score)),
                 max(combined_data$inflammatory_score) - 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$inflammatory_score)),
                 min(combined_data$inflammatory_score) + 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$inflammatory_score)),
                 max(combined_data$inflammatory_score) - 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$inflammatory_score))),
           y = c(max(combined_data$dysbiosis_score) - 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$dysbiosis_score)),
                 max(combined_data$dysbiosis_score) - 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$dysbiosis_score)),
                 min(combined_data$dysbiosis_score) + 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$dysbiosis_score)),
                 min(combined_data$dysbiosis_score) + 0.1 * diff(range(combined_data$dysbiosis_score))),
           label = c("Low Inflammatory\nHigh Dysbiosis", 
                     "High Inflammatory\nHigh Dysbiosis", 
                     "Low Inflammatory\nLow Dysbiosis", 
                     "High Inflammatory\nLow Dysbiosis"),
           size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "gray20") +
  
  # Add labels and title
  labs(title = "Relationship between Dietary Inflammation, Gut Dysbiosis, and Obesity",
       subtitle = paste("Correlation: r =", round(cor(combined_data$inflammatory_score, 
                                                     combined_data$dysbiosis_score, 
                                                     use = "complete.obs"), 2)),
       x = "Dietary Inflammatory Score",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis Score",
       caption = "Dashed lines represent median values. Color indicates combined risk score.") +
  
  # Use a clean theme
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# Add marginal plots to show distributions by obesity status (optional)
# This requires the 'ggExtra' package
# library(ggExtra)
# integrated_plot_with_marginals <- ggMarginal(integrated_plot, 
#                                            type = "density", 
#                                            groupFill = TRUE,
#                                            groupColour = TRUE)

# Display the plot
print(integrated_plot)

# Save the plot
ggsave("inflammatory_dysbiosis_obesity_relationship.png", integrated_plot, 
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Optional: Generate summary statistics for the figure caption
obesity_summary <- aggregate(cbind(inflammatory_score, dysbiosis_score) ~ obesity_status, 
                            data = combined_data, 
                            FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))
print(obesity_summary)

# Calculate percentage of obese participants in each quadrant
combined_data$quadrant <- with(combined_data, 
                              paste0(ifelse(inflammatory_score > median(inflammatory_score), 
                                           "High", "Low"), 
                                    " Inflammatory / ",
                                    ifelse(dysbiosis_score > median(dysbiosis_score), 
                                           "High", "Low"), 
                                    " Dysbiosis"))
quadrant_summary <- table(combined_data$quadrant, combined_data$obesity_status)
quadrant_percentages <- prop.table(quadrant_summary, 1) * 100
print(quadrant_percentages)
```
```{r}
combined_data$obesity_status <- factor(ifelse(combined_data$obesity_binary == 1, "Obese", "Non-obese"))

# Create the integrated plot with clearer obesity differentiation
improved_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, 
                       aes(x = inflammatory_score, 
                           y = dysbiosis_score, 
                           shape = obesity_status,  # Use shape instead of just size
                           size = obesity_status,   # Keep size difference too
                           color = combined_risk)) +
  # Add points with higher alpha for better visibility
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Add contour lines to show density
  stat_density_2d(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray30", alpha = 0.4) +
  
  # Add correlation line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # Color scale
  scale_color_viridis(option = "plasma", name = "Combined Risk\n(Inflammatory + Dysbiosis)") +
  
  # Set point sizes AND shapes for obesity status (this is the key improvement)
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = 2, "Obese" = 4), name = "Obesity Status") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = 16, "Obese" = 18), name = "Obesity Status") +
  
  # Quadrant lines and other elements as before
  geom_hline(yintercept = median(combined_data$dysbiosis_score), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(combined_data$inflammatory_score), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Other elements from original plot...
  labs(title = "Relationship between Dietary Inflammation, Gut Dysbiosis, and Obesity",
       subtitle = paste("Correlation: r =", 
                        round(cor(combined_data$inflammatory_score, combined_data$dysbiosis_score, 
                                 use = "complete.obs"), 2)),
       x = "Dietary Inflammatory Score",
       y = "Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis Score",
       caption = "Dashed lines represent median values. Color indicates combined risk score.\nObese participants shown as larger diamond symbols.") +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Make legend more prominent
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40"))

# Display the improved plot
print(improved_plot)

# Save the improved plot
ggsave("improved_inflammatory_dysbiosis_obesity_relationship.png", improved_plot, 
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Additional analysis to help interpretation
# Calculate mean values by obesity status
obesity_means <- aggregate(cbind(inflammatory_score, dysbiosis_score) ~ obesity_status, 
                          data = combined_data, 
                          FUN = mean)
print(obesity_means)

# Count participants in each quadrant by obesity status
combined_data$quadrant <- paste0(
  ifelse(combined_data$inflammatory_score > median(combined_data$inflammatory_score), 
         "High", "Low"), 
  " Inflammatory / ",
  ifelse(combined_data$dysbiosis_score > median(combined_data$dysbiosis_score), 
         "High", "Low"), 
  " Dysbiosis")

# Create a contingency table
quadrant_obesity_table <- table(combined_data$quadrant, combined_data$obesity_status)
print(quadrant_obesity_table)

# Calculate percentages
quadrant_percentages <- prop.table(quadrant_obesity_table, 1) * 100
print(quadrant_percentages)
```



```{r}
# Boxplot of dysbiosis score
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = factor(obesity_binary), y = dysbiosis_score)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Obesity Status", y = "Dysbiosis Score", title = "Literature-Based Dysbiosis Score")

# Wilcoxon test
wilcox.test(dysbiosis_score ~ obesity_binary, data = merged_data_filter)

```
```{r}
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = factor(obesity_binary), y = dysbiosis_score)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightgray", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(x = "Obesity Status", y = "Dysbiosis Score", title = "Dysbiosis Score by Obesity Status")

```


```{r}
library(effsize)
cliff.delta(dysbiosis_score ~ obesity_binary, data = merged_data_filter)
```

```{r}
library(pROC)
roc_obj <- roc(merged_data_filter$obesity_binary, merged_data_filter$dysbiosis_score)
plot(roc_obj, col = "darkred", print.auc = TRUE)

```
```{r}
library(pROC)

roc_obj <- roc(merged_data_filter$obesity_binary, merged_data_filter$dysbiosis_score)
plot(roc_obj, col = "firebrick", main = "ROC Curve for Dysbiosis Score")
auc(roc_obj)  # print AUC value

```

```{r}
merged_data_filter$obesity_binary_numeric <- ifelse(merged_data_filter$obesity_binary == "Obese", 1, 0)

# Now re-run logistic regression
model <- glm(obesity_binary_numeric ~ dysbiosis_score, data = merged_data_filter, family = "binomial")
summary(model)

```

```{r}
# Load needed packages
library(dplyr)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)

### 1. FILTER FOR MATCHING SPECIES
# These are your trend results from earlier
refined_enriched <- obesity_enriched_trends %>%
  filter(matches_literature == TRUE) %>%
  pull(species)

refined_depleted <- obesity_depleted_trends %>%
  filter(matches_literature == TRUE) %>%
  pull(species)

# Check which ones exist in your dataset
refined_enriched_present <- intersect(refined_enriched, colnames(merged_data_filter))
refined_depleted_present <- intersect(refined_depleted, colnames(merged_data_filter))

### 2. COMPUTE REFINED DYSBIOSIS SCORE
# Compute row-wise sums of enriched and depleted species
refined_enriched_sum <- rowSums(merged_data_filter[, refined_enriched_present], na.rm = TRUE)
refined_depleted_sum <- rowSums(merged_data_filter[, refined_depleted_present], na.rm = TRUE)

# Calculate log-ratio score with pseudocount
merged_data_filter$refined_dysbiosis_score <- log((refined_enriched_sum + 1e-6) / (refined_depleted_sum + 1e-6))

### 3. COMPARE BETWEEN GROUPS
# Optional boxplot
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = factor(obesity_binary), y = refined_dysbiosis_score)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(x = "Obesity Status", y = "Refined Dysbiosis Score", title = "Refined Score by Obesity")

# Wilcoxon test
wilcox.test(refined_dysbiosis_score ~ obesity_binary, data = merged_data_filter)

### 4. LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# Make sure outcome is numeric (0/1)
merged_data_filter$obesity_binary_numeric <- ifelse(merged_data_filter$obesity_binary == "Obese", 1, 0)

# Logistic model
model_refined <- glm(obesity_binary_numeric ~ refined_dysbiosis_score, data = merged_data_filter, family = "binomial")
summary(model_refined)

# Odds ratio
exp(coef(model_refined))

### 5. ROC & AUC
roc_refined <- roc(merged_data_filter$obesity_binary_numeric, merged_data_filter$refined_dysbiosis_score)
plot(roc_refined, col = "darkblue", main = "ROC - Refined Dysbiosis Score", print.auc = TRUE)
auc(roc_refined)

# Optional: get best cutoff and confusion matrix
best_cutoff <- coords(roc_refined, "best", "threshold")
merged_data_filter$refined_predicted_class <- ifelse(merged_data_filter$refined_dysbiosis_score > best_cutoff, 1, 0)

table(Predicted = merged_data_filter$refined_predicted_class,
      Actual = merged_data_filter$obesity_binary_numeric)

```
#machine learning score
```{r}
# Load libraries
library(randomForest)
library(pROC)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)

### 1. Prepare Data

# Ensure Participant_ID is row identifier
X <- species_filtered2 %>%
  column_to_rownames("Participant_ID")

# Match sample order and extract obesity label
y_df <- merged_data_filter %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, obesity_binary_numeric) %>%
  filter(Participant_ID %in% rownames(X))  # ensure only matching samples

# Align rows between X and y
X <- X[match(y_df$Participant_ID, rownames(X)), ]
y <- as.factor(y_df$obesity_binary_numeric)

### 2. Train Random Forest
set.seed(123)
rf_model <- randomForest(x = X, y = y, ntree = 500, importance = TRUE)

### 3. Predict Probabilities (Dysbiosis Score)
rf_scores <- predict(rf_model, X, type = "prob")[, "1"]  # probability of being obese

# Add scores back to merged_data_filter
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  left_join(tibble(Participant_ID = y_df$Participant_ID, rf_dysbiosis_score = rf_scores),
            by = "Participant_ID")

### 4. Evaluate ROC & AUC
roc_rf <- roc(y, rf_scores)
plot(roc_rf, col = "tomato", print.auc = TRUE, main = "Random Forest Dysbiosis Score")
auc_rf <- auc(roc_rf)
print(paste("AUC:", round(auc_rf, 3)))

### 5. View Top Important Features
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Species") %>%
  arrange(desc(MeanDecreaseGini))

head(importance_df, 10)  # Top 10 important species

```
```{r}
set.seed(123)
sample_idx <- sample(1:nrow(X), size = 0.8 * nrow(X))
X_train <- X[sample_idx, ]
X_test <- X[-sample_idx, ]
y_train <- y[sample_idx]
y_test <- y[-sample_idx]

# Train
rf_model <- randomForest(x = X_train, y = y_train, ntree = 500, importance = TRUE)

# Predict on test set
rf_scores_test <- predict(rf_model, X_test, type = "prob")[, "1"]

# Evaluate
roc_test <- roc(y_test, rf_scores_test)
plot(roc_test, col = "steelblue", print.auc = TRUE, main = "Random Forest - Test Set")
auc(roc_test)

```
```{r}
library(caret)

# Create a combined dataframe
rf_input <- data.frame(y = y, X)

# Train with cross-validation
set.seed(123)
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, classProbs = TRUE, summaryFunction = twoClassSummary)

# y must be labeled with strings, not numeric
rf_input$y <- ifelse(rf_input$y == 1, "Obese", "NonObese")

rf_cv <- train(y ~ ., data = rf_input,
               method = "rf",
               metric = "ROC",
               trControl = ctrl)

print(rf_cv)

```






```{r}
# Function to create violin plots for species with log transformation
plot_violin_species_log <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, title) {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if (length(existing_species) == 0) {
    message("No matching species found in the dataset for this plot.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Reshape the data for plotting
  plot_data <- condition_data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(existing_species), condition_column) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -all_of(condition_column), names_to = "species", values_to = "abundance") %>%
    mutate(abundance_log = log10(abundance + 1))  # Log transform (add 1 to avoid log(0))
  
  # Create violin plot
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[condition_column]], y = abundance_log, fill = .data[[condition_column]])) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +  # One plot per species
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = condition_column,
      y = "Log10 Abundance",
      fill = condition_column
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "blue", "Obese" = "orange", "Non-diabetic" = "blue", "Diabetic" = "orange"))
}

# Create separate plots for obesity and diabetes
# Obesity Enriched
obesity_enriched_plot <- plot_violin_species_log(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Enriched Species (Log Transformed)"
)

# Obesity Depleted
obesity_depleted_plot <- plot_violin_species_log(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Depleted Species (Log Transformed)"
)

# Diabetes Enriched
diabetes_enriched_plot <- plot_violin_species_log(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Enriched Species (Log Transformed)"
)

# Diabetes Depleted
diabetes_depleted_plot <- plot_violin_species_log(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Depleted Species (Log Transformed)"
)

# Save plots
ggsave("figure/obesity_enriched_species_log.png", obesity_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_depleted_species_log.png", obesity_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/diabetes_enriched_species_log.png", diabetes_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/diabetes_depleted_species_log.png", diabetes_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

```
## separate ethnicity
```{r}
merged_data_filter
# Function to create violin plots with log transformation, stratification, and consistent color mapping
plot_violin_species_log_ethnic <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, title) {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if (length(existing_species) == 0) {
    message("No matching species found in the dataset for this plot.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Standardize the values in the condition column to match the expected levels
  condition_data <- condition_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[condition_column]]) & FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%  # Remove NA and exclude "O"
    mutate(!!condition_column := case_when(
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_diabetes" ~ "Non-diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "diabetes" ~ "Diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_obese" ~ "Non-obese",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "obese" ~ "Obese",
      TRUE ~ as.character(.data[[condition_column]])
    )) %>%
    mutate(!!condition_column := factor(
      .data[[condition_column]], 
      levels = c("Non-diabetic", "Diabetic", "Non-obese", "Obese")
    ))
  
  # Reshape data for plotting
  plot_data <- condition_data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(existing_species), condition_column, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(all_of(condition_column), FREG5_Ethnic_Group), 
                 names_to = "species", values_to = "abundance") %>%
    mutate(abundance_log = log10(abundance + 1))  # Log transform (add 1 to avoid log(0))
  
  # Debugging: Check levels
  print(levels(plot_data[[condition_column]]))
  
  # Create violin plot
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = abundance_log, fill = .data[[condition_column]])) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = .data[[condition_column]]), 
                width = 0.2, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +  # Adjust jitter without dodge
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +  # One plot per species
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Ethnic Group",
      y = "Log10 Abundance",
      fill = "Condition",
      color = "Condition"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                 "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                  "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
    )
}

# Create separate plots for diabetes and obesity
# Diabetes Enriched
diabetes_enriched_plot <- plot_violin_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Diabetes Depleted
diabetes_depleted_plot <- plot_violin_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Enriched
obesity_enriched_plot <- plot_violin_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Depleted
obesity_depleted_plot <- plot_violin_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Save plots
ggsave("figure/diabetes_enriched_species_log_ethnic.png", diabetes_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/diabetes_depleted_species_log_ethnic.png", diabetes_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_enriched_species_log_ethnic.png", obesity_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_depleted_species_log_ethnic.png", obesity_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

```
## separate ethnic box plot
```{r}
plot_box_species_log_ethnic <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, title) {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if (length(existing_species) == 0) {
    message("No matching species found in the dataset for this plot.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Standardize the values in the condition column to match the expected levels
  condition_data <- condition_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[condition_column]]) & FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%  # Remove NA and exclude "O"
    mutate(!!condition_column := case_when(
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_diabetes" ~ "Non-diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "diabetes" ~ "Diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_obese" ~ "Non-obese",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "obese" ~ "Obese",
      TRUE ~ as.character(.data[[condition_column]])
    )) %>%
    mutate(!!condition_column := factor(
      .data[[condition_column]], 
      levels = c("Non-diabetic", "Diabetic", "Non-obese", "Obese")
    ))
  
  # Reshape data for plotting
  plot_data <- condition_data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(existing_species), condition_column, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(all_of(condition_column), FREG5_Ethnic_Group), 
                 names_to = "species", values_to = "abundance") %>%
    mutate(abundance_log = log10(abundance + 1))  # Log transform (add 1 to avoid log(0))
  
  # Create box plot with dots
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = abundance_log, fill = .data[[condition_column]])) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +  # Remove default outliers
    geom_jitter(aes(color = .data[[condition_column]]), 
                position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75, jitter.width = 0.2),
                size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +  # One plot per species
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Ethnic Group",
      y = "Log10 Abundance",
      fill = "Condition",
      color = "Condition"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                 "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
    )
}

# Create separate plots for diabetes and obesity
# Diabetes Enriched
diabetes_enriched_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Diabetes Depleted
diabetes_depleted_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group", 
  "Diabetes Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Enriched
obesity_enriched_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Depleted
obesity_depleted_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary", 
  "Obesity Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Save plots
ggsave("figure/diabetes_enriched_species_log_ethnic1.png", diabetes_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/diabetes_depleted_species_log_ethnic1.png", diabetes_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_enriched_species_log_ethnic1.png", obesity_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_depleted_species_log_ethnic1.png", obesity_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

# with p value
```{r}
# Function to create box plots with pre-calculated p-values
plot_box_species_log_ethnic <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, 
                                      stratified_results, title) {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if (length(existing_species) == 0) {
    message("No matching species found in the dataset for this plot.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Standardize the values in the condition column
  condition_data <- condition_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[condition_column]]) & FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M")) %>%
    mutate(condition = case_when(
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_diabetes" ~ "Non-diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "diabetes" ~ "Diabetic",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "non_obese" ~ "Non-obese",
      .data[[condition_column]] == "obese" ~ "Obese",
      TRUE ~ as.character(.data[[condition_column]])
    )) %>%
    mutate(condition = factor(
      condition, 
      levels = c("Non-diabetic", "Diabetic", "Non-obese", "Obese")
    ))
  
  # Reshape data for plotting
  plot_data <- condition_data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(existing_species), condition, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(condition, FREG5_Ethnic_Group), 
                 names_to = "species", values_to = "abundance") %>%
    mutate(abundance_log = log10(abundance + 1))
  
  # Prepare p-values for plotting
  p_values <- stratified_results %>%
    group_by(species, FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
    mutate(
      p_label = sprintf("p = %.3f", pvalue),
      # Calculate y position
      y_pos = max(plot_data$abundance_log[plot_data$species == species]) + 0.2
    )
  
  # Create box plot with dots and p-values
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = abundance_log, fill = condition)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = condition), 
                position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75, jitter.width = 0.2),
                size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    # Add p-value annotations
    geom_text(data = p_values,
              aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = y_pos, label = p_label),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
              size = 3) +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Ethnic Group",
      y = "Log10 Abundance",
      fill = "Condition",
      color = "Condition"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-diabetic" = "#4E6691", "Diabetic" = "#B8474D",
                                 "Non-obese" = "#4E6691", "Obese" = "#B8474D")) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
    )
}

# Create separate plots for diabetes and obesity using pre-calculated p-values
# Diabetes Enriched
diabetes_enriched_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group",
  diabetes_enriched_stratified,
  "Diabetes Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Diabetes Depleted
diabetes_depleted_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "diabetes_group",
  diabetes_depleted_stratified,
  "Diabetes Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Enriched
obesity_enriched_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary",
  obesity_enriched_stratified,
  "Obesity Enriched Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Obesity Depleted
obesity_depleted_plot <- plot_box_species_log_ethnic(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter, 
  "obesity_binary",
  obesity_depleted_stratified,
  "Obesity Depleted Species (Log Transformed, Stratified by Ethnic Group)"
)

# Save plots
ggsave("figure/diabetes_enriched_species_log_ethnic_box.png", diabetes_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/diabetes_depleted_species_log_ethnic_box.png", diabetes_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_enriched_species_log_ethnic_box.png", obesity_enriched_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("figure/obesity_depleted_species_log_ethnic_box.png", obesity_depleted_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
diabetes_enriched <- c(
  "Lactobacillus gasseri",
  "Streptococcus mutans",
  "Clostridium citroniae",
  "Clostridium bolteae",
  "Escherichia coli",
  "Bacteroides fragilis",
  "Streptococcus salivarius",  # Example Streptococcus species, you may need to adjust based on your data
  "Streptococcus parasanguinis"
  # Add more species from literature
)

diabetes_depleted <- c(
  "Roseburia intestinalis",
  "Faecalibacterium prausnitzii",
  "Butyrivibrio crossotus",
  "Coprococcus eutactus", 
  "Turicibacter sanguinis",
  "Bacteroides plebeius",
  "Ruminococcus lactaris"
  # Add more species from literature
)

obesity_enriched <- c(
  "Ruminococcus gnavus",
  "Lactobacillus reuteri",
  "Lactobacillus gasseri"
  # Add more species from literature
)

obesity_depleted <- c(
  "Akkermansia muciniphila",
  "Lactobacillus paracasei",
  "Methanobacteriales smithii",
  "Bifidobacterium animalis"
  # Add more species from literature
)

# Function to analyze literature-based species
analyze_literature_species <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column, 
                                     expected_direction = "enriched") {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if(length(existing_species) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Analyze each species
  results <- map_dfr(existing_species, function(species) {
    # Perform Wilcoxon test
    test_result <- wilcox.test(
      get(species) ~ get(condition_column), 
      data = condition_data
    )
    
    # Calculate fold change
    means <- condition_data %>%
      group_by(get(condition_column)) %>%
      summarise(mean_val = mean(get(species))) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = `get(condition_column)`, values_from = mean_val)
    
    # Assuming binary columns like diabetes/non-diabetes or obese/non-obese
    if(expected_direction == "enriched") {
      log2FC <- log2(means[[2]] / means[[1]])  # condition/control
    } else {
      log2FC <- -log2(means[[2]] / means[[1]])  # negative of condition/control
    }
    
    data.frame(
      species = species,
      pvalue = test_result$p.value,
      log2FoldChange = log2FC,
      matches_literature = sign(log2FC) == if(expected_direction == "enriched") 1 else -1
    )
  })
  
  results$padj <- p.adjust(results$pvalue, method = "BH")
  return(results)
}

# Analyze diabetes-associated species
diabetes_enriched_results <- analyze_literature_species(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  "enriched"
)

diabetes_depleted_results <- analyze_literature_species(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  "depleted"
)

# Analyze obesity-associated species
obesity_enriched_results <- analyze_literature_species(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  "enriched"
)

obesity_depleted_results <- analyze_literature_species(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  "depleted"
)

# Create summary visualization
library(gridExtra)

plot_literature_comparison <- function(results, title) {
  if(is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  ggplot(results, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
    geom_point(aes(color = matches_literature)) +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = species)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue")) +
    labs(title = title) +
    theme_minimal()
}

# Create plots
p1 <- plot_literature_comparison(diabetes_enriched_results, "Diabetes Enriched Species")
p2 <- plot_literature_comparison(diabetes_depleted_results, "Diabetes Depleted Species")
p3 <- plot_literature_comparison(obesity_enriched_results, "Obesity Enriched Species")
p4 <- plot_literature_comparison(obesity_depleted_results, "Obesity Depleted Species")

# Arrange plots
g = grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2)
ggsave("figure/microbiota_diabete_obesity2.png", g, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
# Create summary table
create_summary_table <- function(results, condition) {
  if(is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  results %>%
    mutate(
      significant = padj < 0.1,
      agrees_with_literature = matches_literature & significant,
      condition = condition
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(species, significant, agrees_with_literature, condition)
}

summary_table <- bind_rows(
  create_summary_table(diabetes_enriched_results, "Diabetes Enriched"),
  create_summary_table(diabetes_depleted_results, "Diabetes Depleted"),
  create_summary_table(obesity_enriched_results, "Obesity Enriched"),
  create_summary_table(obesity_depleted_results, "Obesity Depleted")
)

print(summary_table)
```


```{r}
analyze_literature_species_stratified <- function(species_list, condition_data, condition_column,
                                                ethnic_groups = c("C", "I", "M"),
                                                expected_direction = "enriched") {
  
  # Filter for species that exist in your dataset
  existing_species <- intersect(species_list, colnames(condition_data))
  
  if(length(existing_species) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create a list to store results for each ethnic group
  results_list <- map(ethnic_groups, function(ethnic_group) {
    # Filter data for current ethnic group
    ethnic_data <- condition_data %>%
      filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == ethnic_group)
    
    # Skip if no data for this ethnic group
    if(nrow(ethnic_data) == 0) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    # Analyze each species within this ethnic group
    results <- map_dfr(existing_species, function(species) {
      # Check if there's enough data in each condition group
      condition_counts <- table(ethnic_data[[condition_column]])
      if(any(condition_counts < 2)) {
        return(data.frame(
          species = species,
          pvalue = NA,
          log2FoldChange = NA,
          matches_literature = NA
        ))
      }
      
      # Perform Wilcoxon test
      tryCatch({
        test_result <- wilcox.test(
          get(species) ~ get(condition_column), 
          data = ethnic_data
        )
        
        # Calculate fold change
        means <- ethnic_data %>%
          group_by(get(condition_column)) %>%
          summarise(mean_val = mean(get(species))) %>%
          pivot_wider(names_from = `get(condition_column)`, values_from = mean_val)
        
        if(expected_direction == "enriched") {
          log2FC <- log2(means[[2]] / means[[1]])  # condition/control
        } else {
          log2FC <- -log2(means[[2]] / means[[1]])  # negative of condition/control
        }
        
        data.frame(
          species = species,
          pvalue = test_result$p.value,
          log2FoldChange = log2FC,
          matches_literature = sign(log2FC) == if(expected_direction == "enriched") 1 else -1
        )
      }, error = function(e) {
        data.frame(
          species = species,
          pvalue = NA,
          log2FoldChange = NA,
          matches_literature = NA
        )
      })
    })
    
    # Add FDR correction within each ethnic group
    results$padj <- p.adjust(results$pvalue, method = "BH")
    results$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- ethnic_group
    
    return(results)
  })
  
  # Combine results from all ethnic groups
  final_results <- bind_rows(results_list)
  return(final_results)
}

# Usage example for diabetes analysis
diabetes_enriched_stratified <- analyze_literature_species_stratified(
  diabetes_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  c("C", "I", "M"),
  "enriched"
)

diabetes_depleted_stratified <- analyze_literature_species_stratified(
  diabetes_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "diabetes_group",
  c("C", "I", "M"),
  "depleted"
)

# Usage example for obesity analysis
obesity_enriched_stratified <- analyze_literature_species_stratified(
  obesity_enriched, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  c("C", "I", "M"),
  "enriched"
)

obesity_depleted_stratified <- analyze_literature_species_stratified(
  obesity_depleted, 
  merged_data_filter,
  "obesity_binary",
  c("C", "I", "M"),
  "depleted"
)

# Optional: Create summary tables for each condition
# create_summary_table <- function(results_data) {
#   results_data %>%
#     filter(!is.na(pvalue)) %>%
#     group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
#     summarise(
#       total_species = n(),
#       significant_species = sum(padj < 0.05),
#       matching_direction = sum(matches_literature & padj < 0.05)
#     )
# }
# 
# # Example usage of summary table
# diabetes_enriched_summary <- create_summary_table(diabetes_enriched_stratified)
# diabetes_depleted_summary <- create_summary_table(diabetes_depleted_stratified)
# obesity_enriched_summary <- create_summary_table(obesity_enriched_stratified)
# obesity_depleted_summary <- create_summary_table(obesity_depleted_stratified)
```


```{r}
diabetes_enriched_stratified
diabetes_depleted_stratified
obesity_enriched_stratified
obesity_depleted_stratified
```
##FB ratio
```{r}
firmicutes_species <- grep("^(Clostridium|Ruminococcus|Lactobacillus|Bacillus|Enterococcus|Streptococcus|Staphylococcus|Eubacterium|Roseburia|Faecalibacterium|Butyrivibrio)", 
                         colnames(merged_data_filter), value = TRUE)
bacteroidetes_species <- grep("^(Bacteroides|Prevotella|Porphyromonas|Alistipes|Parabacteroides)", 
                            colnames(merged_data_filter), value = TRUE)
# Calculate sum of relative abundances for each phylum
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(
    Firmicutes_total = rowSums(select(., all_of(firmicutes_species))),
    Bacteroidetes_total = rowSums(select(., all_of(bacteroidetes_species))),
    FB_ratio = Firmicutes_total / Bacteroidetes_total
  )
```

## regression
```{r}

# firmicutes_species <- grep("^(Clostridium|Ruminococcus|Lactobacillus|Bacillus|Enterococcus|Streptococcus|Staphylococcus|Eubacterium|Roseburia|Faecalibacterium|Butyrivibrio)", 
#                          colnames(merged_data_filter), value = TRUE)
# 
# bacteroidetes_species <- grep("^(Bacteroides|Prevotella|Porphyromonas|Alistipes|Parabacteroides)", 
#                             colnames(merged_data_filter), value = TRUE)
```




```{r}
# First normalize the counts to relative abundances
# Get species columns (all except the first column from species_filtered2)
merged_data_fb <- species1 %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

species_cols <- colnames(species1)[-1]

# Create normalized abundances
merged_data_fb <- merged_data_fb %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(species_cols), 
                ~./rowSums(select(merged_data_fb, all_of(species_cols))), 
                .names = "{.col}_norm"))

# Now get the normalized species for each phylum
firmicutes_species_norm <- grep("^(Clostridium|Ruminococcus|Lactobacillus|Bacillus|Enterococcus|Streptococcus|Staphylococcus|Eubacterium|Roseburia|Faecalibacterium|Butyrivibrio).*_norm$", 
                         colnames(merged_data_fb), value = TRUE)

bacteroidetes_species_norm <- grep("^(Bacteroides|Prevotella|Porphyromonas|Alistipes|Parabacteroides).*_norm$", 
                            colnames(merged_data_fb), value = TRUE)

# Calculate FB ratio using normalized abundances
merged_data_fb <- merged_data_fb %>%
  mutate(
    Firmicutes_total = rowSums(select(., all_of(firmicutes_species_norm))),
    Bacteroidetes_total = rowSums(select(., all_of(bacteroidetes_species_norm))),
    FB_ratio = Firmicutes_total / Bacteroidetes_total
  )
```


```{r}
merged_data_fb <- merged_data_fb[merged_data_fb$FB_ratio <= 5, ]
# Create the plot with normalized data
ggplot(merged_data_fb, aes(x = BMI, y = FB_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship between BMI and FB Ratio (Using Normalized Abundances)",
    x = "BMI",
    y = "FB Ratio"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Check the regression
model <- lm(FB_ratio ~ BMI, data = merged_data_fb)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
# Create a BMI category variable
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(BMI_category = ifelse(BMI < 25, "Normal Weight (BMI < 25)", "Obese (BMI ≥ 25)"))

# Create the plot with two regression lines
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = BMI, y = FB_ratio, color = BMI_category)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Normal Weight (BMI < 25)" = "blue", 
                               "Overweight/Obese (BMI ≥ 25)" = "red")) +
  labs(
    title = "FB Ratio vs BMI by Weight Category",
    x = "BMI",
    y = "FB Ratio",
    color = "BMI Category"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# Perform separate regression analyses
# For BMI < 25
model_normal <- lm(FB_ratio ~ BMI, 
                  data = merged_data_filter[merged_data_filter$BMI < 25,])
print("Regression results for BMI < 25:")
summary(model_normal)

# For BMI ≥ 25
model_overweight <- lm(FB_ratio ~ BMI, 
                      data = merged_data_filter[merged_data_filter$BMI >= 25,])
print("Regression results for BMI ≥ 25:")
summary(model_overweight)
```


##box plot
```{r}
# Log-transform FB_ratio to reduce the influence of extreme values
merged_data_filter <- merged_data_filter %>%
  mutate(FB_ratio_log = log10(FB_ratio + 0.001))  # Add small constant to handle zeros

# Re-run Wilcoxon test with log-transformed values
wilcox_test_result <- wilcox.test(FB_ratio_log ~ obesity_binary, data = merged_data_filter)
print(wilcox_test_result)

# Create a boxplot with log-transformed data
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio_log)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Exclude outliers from boxplot display
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, color = "blue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Log-Transformed Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes Ratio by Obesity Status",
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "Log10(F/B Ratio)"
  )

# Optional: Visualize the untransformed data but highlight the extreme value
ggplot(merged_data_filter, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, color = "blue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes Ratio by Obesity Status",
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "F/B Ratio"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10", breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 8000))  # Log scale for clarity

```






##stratify by ethnic
```{r}
# Filter out the "O" ethnic group
ethnic_group_data_filtered <- ethnic_group_data %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group != "O")

# Re-plot with properly aligned jitter points
ggplot(ethnic_group_data_filtered, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio, color = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group), alpha = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA) +  # Boxplot without outlier symbols
  geom_jitter(aes(x = obesity_binary), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +  # Ensure jitter aligns with box groups
  geom_point(data = subset(ethnic_group_data_filtered, FB_ratio > 1000), 
             aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio), 
             color = "red", size = 3, shape = 8) +  # Highlight extreme value
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes Ratio by Obesity Status and Ethnic Group",
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "F/B Ratio (Untransformed)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10", breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 8000)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("C" = "blue", "I" = "green", "M" = "orange")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("C" = "blue", "I" = "green", "M" = "orange"))

```
```{r}
# Filter out the "O" ethnic group
ethnic_group_data_filtered <- ethnic_group_data %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group != "O")

# Re-plot: Group by ethnic group, compare obese vs non-obese
ggplot(ethnic_group_data_filtered, aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio, color = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = obesity_binary), alpha = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA) +  # Boxplot without outlier symbols
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +  # Jitter points for visualization
  geom_point(data = subset(ethnic_group_data_filtered, FB_ratio > 1000), 
             aes(x = obesity_binary, y = FB_ratio), 
             color = "red", size = 3, shape = 8) +  # Highlight extreme value
  facet_wrap(~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group, scales = "free") +  # Facet by ethnic group
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes Ratio by Ethnic Group and Obesity Status",
    x = "Obesity Status",
    y = "F/B Ratio (Untransformed)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10", breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 8000)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "blue", "Obese" = "orange")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-obese" = "blue", "Obese" = "orange"))

```

```{r}
# Calculate the distribution of obesity_binary by ethnic group
obesity_dist <- merged_data_filter %>%
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, obesity_binary) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  # Calculate percentages within each ethnic group
  group_by(FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100)


obesity_dist <- obesity_dist %>%
  mutate(obesity_binary = factor(obesity_binary, levels = c("Obese", "Non-obese")))

# Create the bar plot with updated stacking order
ggplot(obesity_dist, aes(x = FREG5_Ethnic_Group, y = count, fill = obesity_binary)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Obese" = "#B8474D", "Non-obese" = "4E6691")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Obesity Status by Ethnic Group",
    x = "Ethnic Group",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Obesity Status"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )


```
